translator: Claude (Opus 4.7) — original, plain-modern English rendering from the Sanskrit
notes: A plain, modern English translation meant for ordinary readers with no background in Sanskrit or the Gita. Faithful to meaning but free of archaic style. Speakers are named directly (Krishna, Arjuna, Sanjaya). Central Sanskrit terms (dharma, karma, yoga, atman, Brahman, guna) are translated in context and kept untranslated only where the English flattens the meaning.

verses.sanskrit:
धृतराष्ट्र उवाच

धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः।

मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय।।1.1।।
verses.translation:
Dhritarashtra said:
Sanjaya, tell me — my sons and the sons of Pandu gathered on the sacred field of Kurukshetra, ready to fight. What did they do?

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा।

आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत्।।1.2।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Seeing the Pandava army drawn up for battle, King Duryodhana went to his teacher Drona and said:

verses.sanskrit:
पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणामाचार्य महतीं चमूम्।

व्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता।।1.3।।
verses.translation: "Look, teacher, at this great Pandava army, arranged by your own brilliant student, Drupada's son."

verses.sanskrit:
अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि।

युयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथः।।1.4।।
verses.translation: "Here are heroes, mighty archers — a match in battle for Bhima and Arjuna — Yuyudhana, Virata, and Drupada, the great charioteer;"

verses.sanskrit:
धृष्टकेतुश्चेकितानः काशिराजश्च वीर्यवान्।

पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च शैब्यश्च नरपुङ्गवः।।1.5।।
verses.translation: "Dhrishtaketu, Chekitana, and the brave king of Kashi; Purujit, Kuntibhoja, and Shaibya, a leader of men;"

verses.sanskrit:
युधामन्युश्च विक्रान्त उत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान्।

सौभद्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व एव महारथाः।।1.6।।
verses.translation: "the valiant Yudhamanyu, the powerful Uttamaujas, Subhadra's son, and Draupadi's sons — all great warriors."

verses.sanskrit:
अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम।

नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते।।1.7।।
verses.translation: "Now let me name, O best of the twice-born, the best warriors on our side, the leaders of my army — listen:"

verses.sanskrit:
भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जयः।

अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च।।1.8।।
verses.translation: "You yourself, Bhishma, Karna, Kripa — all victorious in battle; Ashvatthama, Vikarna, and Somadatta's son;"

verses.sanskrit:
अन्ये च बहवः शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविताः।

नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणाः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः।।1.9।।
verses.translation: "and many other heroes, skilled with many weapons, all battle-tested and ready to die for me."

verses.sanskrit:
अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम्।

पर्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम्।।1.10।।
verses.translation: "Our army, led by Bhishma, is immense; theirs, led by Bhima, is limited."

verses.sanskrit:
अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः।

भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व एव हि।।1.11।।
verses.translation: "So all of you, holding your positions at every point in the formation, protect Bhishma above everyone else."

verses.sanskrit:
तस्य संजनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्धः पितामहः।

सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चैः शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान्।।1.12।।
verses.translation: Then, to cheer Duryodhana, the old grandfather of the Kurus, Bhishma, roared like a lion and blew his conch loudly.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः।

सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत्।।1.13।।
verses.translation: Then conches, kettledrums, tabors, trumpets, and horns blared out all at once — a tremendous noise.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ।

माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः।।1.14।।
verses.translation: Then, standing in their great chariot yoked to white horses, Krishna and Arjuna blew their divine conches.

verses.sanskrit:
पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनंजयः।

पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदरः।।1.15।।
verses.translation: Krishna blew his conch Panchajanya; Arjuna blew Devadatta; and Bhima of terrifying deeds blew the great conch Paundra.

verses.sanskrit:
अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः।

नकुलः सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ।।1.16।।
verses.translation: King Yudhishthira blew Anantavijaya; Nakula and Sahadeva blew Sughosha and Manipushpaka.

verses.sanskrit:
काश्यश्च परमेष्वासः शिखण्डी च महारथः।

धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजितः।।1.17।।
verses.translation: The king of Kashi, a supreme archer; Shikhandi, the great warrior; Dhrishtadyumna, Virata, and the unconquered Satyaki;

verses.sanskrit:
द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः पृथिवीपते।

सौभद्रश्च महाबाहुः शङ्खान्दध्मुः पृथक्पृथक्।।1.18।।
verses.translation: Drupada, the sons of Draupadi, and Subhadra's mighty son — O king, each of them blew his own conch.

verses.sanskrit:
स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत्।

नभश्च पृथिवीं चैव तुमुलो व्यनुनादयन्।।1.19।।
verses.translation: That tremendous sound, echoing through sky and earth, shattered the hearts of Dhritarashtra's sons.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ व्यवस्थितान् दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रान्कपिध्वजः।

प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसंपाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डवः।।1.20।।
verses.translation: Then, seeing the enemy drawn up, with weapons about to clash, Arjuna, whose banner carried the image of Hanuman, raised his bow and said to Krishna:

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते।

सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत।।1.21।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, place my chariot between the two armies,

verses.sanskrit:
यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान्।

कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन्रणसमुद्यमे।।1.22।।
verses.translation: so I can see the men standing here eager to fight — those I'm about to face in this battle.

verses.sanskrit:
योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः।

धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः।।1.23।।
verses.translation: I want to see who has gathered here to fight, those who came to serve the evil-minded Duryodhana.

verses.sanskrit:
संजय उवाच

एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत।

सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम्।।1.24।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Asked this by Arjuna, Krishna drove the splendid chariot into the space between the two armies,

verses.sanskrit:
भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखतः सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम्।

उवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति।।1.25।।
verses.translation: facing Bhishma, Drona, and all the kings of the earth, and said: "Arjuna, look at these Kurus gathered here."

verses.sanskrit:
तत्रापश्यत्स्थितान्पार्थः पितृ़नथ पितामहान्।

आचार्यान्मातुलान्भ्रातृ़न्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्सखींस्तथा।।1.26।।
verses.translation: Arjuna looked and saw, standing in both armies, fathers and grandfathers, teachers, uncles, brothers, sons, grandsons, and friends,

verses.sanskrit:
श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि।

तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेयः सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान्।।1.27।।
verses.translation: fathers-in-law and companions. Seeing all these relatives lined up for battle, Arjuna was overwhelmed with pity, and said in sorrow:

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

कृपया परयाऽऽविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत्।

दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम्।।1.28।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, seeing my own family here, ready to fight, my limbs give way and my mouth runs dry.

verses.sanskrit:
सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति।

वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते।।1.29।।
verses.translation: My body shakes, my hair stands on end, my bow Gandiva slips from my hand, and my skin is burning.

verses.sanskrit:
गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते।

न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः।।1.30।।
verses.translation: I can't stand steady; my mind is reeling. I see bad omens, Krishna.

verses.sanskrit:
निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव।

न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे।।1.31।।
verses.translation: I see nothing good in killing my own people in battle. I don't want victory, or a kingdom, or pleasures, Krishna.

verses.sanskrit:
न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च।

किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा।।1.32।।
verses.translation: What use is a kingdom to us, Govinda? What use are enjoyments or even life, when the very people for whose sake we want them

verses.sanskrit:
येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च।

त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च।।1.33।।
verses.translation: are standing here ready to fight, giving up their lives and wealth — teachers, fathers, sons, grandfathers,

verses.sanskrit:
आचार्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः।

मातुलाः श्चशुराः पौत्राः श्यालाः सम्बन्धिनस्तथा।।1.34।।
verses.translation: uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law, other relatives? Krishna, I don't want to kill them, even if they kill me — not for all three worlds, much less for this earth.

verses.sanskrit:
एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन।

अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते।।1.35।।
verses.translation: What joy would we get, Krishna, from killing Dhritarashtra's sons? Only sin would stain us, even though they are the aggressors.

verses.sanskrit:
निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्दन।

पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः।।1.36।।
verses.translation: So it's wrong for us to kill Dhritarashtra's sons — our own relatives. How could we be happy, Krishna, after killing our own family?

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान्।

स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिनः स्याम माधव।।1.37।।
verses.translation: Even if they, with minds clouded by greed, see nothing wrong in destroying a family or betraying friends —

verses.sanskrit:
यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः।

कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम्।।1.38।।
verses.translation: why shouldn't we, who clearly see the evil of destroying a family, turn away from this sin?

verses.sanskrit:
कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम्।

कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन।।1.39।।
verses.translation: When a family is destroyed, its ancient traditions are lost. When tradition is lost, lawlessness takes over the whole family.

verses.sanskrit:
कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्माः सनातनाः।

धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत।।1.40।।
verses.translation: When lawlessness dominates, the women become corrupted; and when women are corrupted, social order breaks down.

verses.sanskrit:
अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः।

स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः।।1.41।।
verses.translation: This confusion leads the destroyers of the family — and the family itself — to ruin, for the ancestors, deprived of the offerings due to them, also fall.

verses.sanskrit:
सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च।

पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः।।1.42।।
verses.translation: Through the sins of these family-destroyers, which cause the collapse of social order, the ancient customs of caste and family are uprooted.

verses.sanskrit:
दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः।

उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्माः कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वताः।।1.43।।
verses.translation: Krishna, I've heard that people whose family traditions are destroyed are condemned to suffer for a long time.

verses.sanskrit:
उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन।

नरकेऽनियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम।।1.44।।
verses.translation: How terrible — we're ready to commit a great sin, prepared to kill our own relatives out of greed for a kingdom.

verses.sanskrit:
अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम्।

यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः।।1.45।।
verses.translation: It would be better for me if the sons of Dhritarashtra, weapons in hand, killed me in battle unarmed and not fighting back.

verses.sanskrit:
यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः।

धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत्।।1.46।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Having said this on the battlefield, Arjuna cast aside his bow and arrows and sank down on the seat of his chariot, his mind crushed by grief.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

एवमुक्त्वाऽर्जुनः संख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत्।

विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः।।1.47।।
verses.translation: (In editions that count this as a separate verse:) So Arjuna, overcome with sorrow, threw down his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot seat, his heart broken with grief.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

तं तथा कृपयाऽविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम्।

विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः।।2.1।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
To Arjuna — overcome with pity, eyes brimming and downcast, mind distressed — Krishna spoke these words:

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

कुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम्।

अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन।।2.2।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Where has this weakness come from, Arjuna, at such a critical moment? It doesn't suit a noble man. It won't bring heaven, and it won't bring honor.

verses.sanskrit:
क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते।

क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप।।2.3।।
verses.translation: Don't give in to this cowardice — it isn't you. Shake off this petty weakness of heart and stand up, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

कथं भीष्ममहं संख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन।

इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन।।2.4।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, how can I shoot arrows at Bhishma and Drona on the battlefield? They deserve my respect, not my weapons.

verses.sanskrit:
गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान्

श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके।

हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैव

भुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान्।।2.5।।
verses.translation: Better to live by begging in this world than to kill these great teachers. If I kill them — even though they seek their own gain — everything I enjoy will be stained with their blood.

verses.sanskrit:
न चैतद्विद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयो

यद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः।

यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषाम

स्तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्राः।।2.6।।
verses.translation: And I don't know which is better — that we defeat them or they defeat us. Those very sons of Dhritarashtra stand there, and if I kill them I won't want to live.

verses.sanskrit:
कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः

पृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसंमूढचेताः।

यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्िचतं ब्रूहि तन्मे

शिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम्।।2.7।।
verses.translation: My mind is clouded by weak pity; I'm confused about what's right. Tell me clearly what's best for me. I am your student. Teach me — I take refuge in you.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्या

द्यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम्।

अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धम्

राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम्।।2.8।।
verses.translation: I see nothing that can remove this grief drying up my senses — not even a prosperous kingdom on earth without rivals, nor lordship over the gods.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तप।

न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह।।2.9।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Having said this, Arjuna — the conqueror of sleep, the scorcher of enemies — told Krishna "I will not fight," and fell silent.

verses.sanskrit:
तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत।

सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः।।2.10।।
verses.translation: O king, to Arjuna grieving between the two armies, Krishna, as if smiling, spoke these words:

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

अशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे।

गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः।।2.11।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
You grieve for those who should not be grieved for — and yet you speak words of wisdom. The wise grieve for neither the living nor the dead.

verses.sanskrit:
न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः।

न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम्।।2.12।।
verses.translation: There was never a time when I did not exist, nor you, nor these kings. Nor will any of us cease to exist in the future.

verses.sanskrit:
देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा।

तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति।।2.13।।
verses.translation: Just as the soul within this body passes through childhood, youth, and old age, it passes into another body after death. The wise are not troubled by this.

verses.sanskrit:
मात्रास्पर्शास्तु कौन्तेय शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः।

आगमापायिनोऽनित्यास्तांस्तितिक्षस्व भारत।।2.14।।
verses.translation: Contact with the world brings cold and heat, pleasure and pain — they come and go, they don't last. Endure them, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ।

समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते।।2.15।।
verses.translation: The person these things don't disturb — the one steady in pleasure and pain — that wise person is ready for immortality.

verses.sanskrit:
नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः।

उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः।।2.16।।
verses.translation: What isn't real can never truly exist; what is real can never stop existing. Those who see the truth see both clearly.

verses.sanskrit:
अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम्।

विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित् कर्तुमर्हति।।2.17।।
verses.translation: Know that what pervades all this is indestructible. No one can destroy what does not pass away.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः।

अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत।।2.18।।
verses.translation: These bodies have an end — but the embodied one within them is eternal, indestructible, beyond measure. So fight, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम्।

उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते।।2.19।।
verses.translation: Anyone who thinks the soul kills, or that the soul is killed — neither understands. The soul does not kill and is not killed.

verses.sanskrit:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचि

न्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः।

अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो

न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे।।2.20।।
verses.translation: The soul is never born and never dies. Once it exists, it does not cease to be. Unborn, eternal, everlasting, ancient — it is not killed when the body is killed.

verses.sanskrit:
वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम्।

कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम्।।2.21।।
verses.translation: Arjuna, if someone knows the soul is indestructible, eternal, unborn, unchanging — how can such a person kill anyone or cause anyone to kill?

verses.sanskrit:
वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहाय

नवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि।

तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णा

न्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही।।2.22।।
verses.translation: Just as a person throws away worn-out clothes and puts on new ones, the soul throws away worn-out bodies and takes on new ones.

verses.sanskrit:
नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः।

न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः।।2.23।।
verses.translation: Weapons cannot cut the soul, fire cannot burn it, water cannot wet it, wind cannot dry it.

verses.sanskrit:
अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च।

नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः।।2.24।।
verses.translation: It cannot be cut, burned, wetted, or dried. It is eternal, everywhere, stable, unmoving, forever.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्तोऽयमचिन्त्योऽयमविकार्योऽयमुच्यते।

तस्मादेवं विदित्वैनं नानुशोचितुमर्हसि।।2.25।।
verses.translation: The soul is beyond the reach of the senses, beyond thought, unchanging. Knowing this, you shouldn't grieve.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम्।

तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि।।2.26।।
verses.translation: And even if you think the soul is constantly being born and dying, you still have no reason to grieve, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च।

तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि।।2.27।।
verses.translation: Whoever is born must die; whoever dies must be born again. There's no point grieving over the unavoidable.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत।

अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना।।2.28।।
verses.translation: Beings are unseen before they appear, visible in the middle, and unseen again when they go. Why grieve about this?

verses.sanskrit:
आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेन

माश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः।

आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्यः श्रृणोति

श्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित्।।2.29।।
verses.translation: Some look at the soul as a wonder; some speak of it as a wonder; others hear about it as a wonder — and even after hearing, no one truly knows it.

verses.sanskrit:
देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत।

तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि।।2.30।।
verses.translation: The one within every body is eternal and indestructible, Arjuna. So there's no reason to grieve for any living being.

verses.sanskrit:
स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि।

धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाछ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते।।2.31।।
verses.translation: Look at it from the standpoint of your own duty, and you won't hesitate. For a warrior there is nothing better than a righteous battle.

verses.sanskrit:
यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम्।

सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम्।।2.32।।
verses.translation: Fortunate are the warriors, Arjuna, who are offered a battle like this that comes of its own accord — an open door to heaven.

verses.sanskrit:
अथ चैत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं संग्रामं न करिष्यसि।

ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि।।2.33।।
verses.translation: But if you refuse to fight this righteous battle, you'll abandon your duty and your honor — and you'll earn only sin.

verses.sanskrit:
अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम्।

संभावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते।।2.34।।
verses.translation: People will speak of your lasting disgrace, and for an honored man, dishonor is worse than death.

verses.sanskrit:
भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः।

येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम्।।2.35।।
verses.translation: The great warriors will think you withdrew out of fear, and those who once respected you will hold you in contempt.

verses.sanskrit:
अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून् वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः।

निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दुःखतरं नु किम्।।2.36।।
verses.translation: Your enemies will say cruel things, mocking your strength. What could be more painful than that?

verses.sanskrit:
हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम्।

तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः।।2.37।।
verses.translation: If you're killed, you'll reach heaven; if you win, you'll rule the earth. So stand up, Arjuna, with the resolve to fight.

verses.sanskrit:
सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ।

ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि।।2.38।।
verses.translation: Treat pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat as equal, and then fight. This way you won't incur any sin.

verses.sanskrit:
एषा तेऽभिहिता सांख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां श्रृणु।

बुद्ध्यायुक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि।।2.39।।
verses.translation: This wisdom I've described is from the standpoint of knowledge; now hear it from the standpoint of action (yoga). Armed with this, you'll break free from the bondage of karma.

verses.sanskrit:
नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते।

स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात्।।2.40।।
verses.translation: On this path, no effort is wasted, no progress is undone. Even a little of this practice protects you from great fear.

verses.sanskrit:
व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन।

बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम्।।2.41।।
verses.translation: On this path, Arjuna, the mind is focused — one resolution, one direction. But the minds of the unresolved scatter endlessly in many directions.

verses.sanskrit:
यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः।

वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः।।2.42।।
verses.translation: The unwise get carried away with the flowery passages of the Vedas, declaring "there's nothing more than this,"

verses.sanskrit:
कामात्मानः स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम्।

क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति।।2.43।।
verses.translation: full of desire, aiming at heaven, offering rituals for rebirth and results — loaded with specific ceremonies chasing pleasure and power.

verses.sanskrit:
भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम्।

व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते।।2.44।।
verses.translation: For people attached to pleasure and power, their minds swept away by such talk, the focused, meditative mind never settles.

verses.sanskrit:
त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन।

निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान्।।2.45।।
verses.translation: The Vedas deal with the three qualities of nature (sattva, rajas, tamas). Rise above them, Arjuna — beyond the pairs of opposites, steady in purity, free from concerns of gain and loss, self-possessed.

verses.sanskrit:
यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वतः संप्लुतोदके।

तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः।।2.46।।
verses.translation: A well is of little use when water is flooding everywhere. Similarly, all the Vedas are of little use to the one who truly knows.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन।

मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि।।2.47।।
verses.translation: You have a right to your action, but never to its results. Don't let the results be your motivation, and don't cling to inaction either.

verses.sanskrit:
योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय।

सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते।।2.48।।
verses.translation: Do your work steadily, Arjuna, letting go of attachment, indifferent to success and failure. This evenness of mind is what yoga means.

verses.sanskrit:
दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय।

बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः।।2.49।।
verses.translation: Action driven by results is far inferior to action done with wisdom. Take refuge in wisdom. Pitiable are those who act just for results.

verses.sanskrit:
बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते।

तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम्।।2.50।।
verses.translation: A person whose mind is steady in wisdom drops both good and bad deeds here and now. So commit yourself to yoga. Yoga is skill in action.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः।

जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम्।।2.51।।
verses.translation: The wise, their minds steady, let go of the fruits of action, are freed from the bondage of rebirth, and reach a state beyond all suffering.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति।

तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च।।2.52।।
verses.translation: When your mind cuts through the tangle of delusion, you'll stop caring about what you've heard or have yet to hear.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला।

समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि।।2.53।।
verses.translation: When your mind, once confused by scriptures, stands firm and unwavering in deep stillness, you will have attained yoga.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

स्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव।

स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम्।।2.54।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, what is a person of steady wisdom like — one established in deep stillness? How does such a person speak? How do they sit and move?

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

प्रजहाति यदा कामान् सर्वान् पार्थ मनोगतान्।

आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते।।2.55।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
When a person lets go of all the desires of the mind and finds contentment in the Self alone, they are called a person of steady wisdom.

verses.sanskrit:
दुःखेष्वनुद्विग्नमनाः सुखेषु विगतस्पृहः।

वीतरागभयक्रोधः स्थितधीर्मुनिरुच्यते।।2.56।।
verses.translation: Not shaken by sorrow, not craving pleasures, free from desire, fear, and anger — such a person is called a sage of steady mind.

verses.sanskrit:
यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम्।

नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता।।2.57।।
verses.translation: Unattached to anything, neither pleased by good nor upset by bad — that person's wisdom is firmly established.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः।

इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता।।2.58।।
verses.translation: When a person can pull back their senses from objects the way a tortoise draws in its limbs — their wisdom is firmly established.

verses.sanskrit:
विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः।

रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते।।2.59।।
verses.translation: Objects fall away from the person who abstains, but the craving for them lingers — until they experience what is higher. Then even the craving goes.

verses.sanskrit:
यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः।

इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः।।2.60।।
verses.translation: The restless senses, Arjuna, can forcibly carry off the mind even of a wise person who is trying.

verses.sanskrit:
तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः।

वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता।।2.61।।
verses.translation: So a person should hold all the senses in check and sit focused, devoted to Me. When the senses are under control, wisdom is firmly established.

verses.sanskrit:
ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते।

सङ्गात् संजायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते।।2.62।।
verses.translation: Dwelling on sense objects breeds attachment to them; attachment breeds desire; desire breeds anger.

verses.sanskrit:
क्रोधाद्भवति संमोहः संमोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः।

स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति।।2.63।।
verses.translation: Anger breeds delusion; delusion breeds loss of memory; loss of memory breeds destruction of reason; and with reason destroyed, the person is lost.

verses.sanskrit:
रागद्वेषवियुक्तैस्तु विषयानिन्द्रियैश्चरन्।

आत्मवश्यैर्विधेयात्मा प्रसादमधिगच्छति।।2.64।।
verses.translation: But a disciplined person moving among sense objects, senses under control, free from attachment and aversion, guided by the Self — attains inner peace.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते।

प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते।।2.65।।
verses.translation: In peace, all sorrows are destroyed. For the peaceful-minded, steady wisdom quickly takes root.

verses.sanskrit:
नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना।

न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम्।।2.66।।
verses.translation: The undisciplined have no understanding and no meditation. Without meditation, no peace; without peace, how can there be happiness?

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते।

तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि।।2.67।।
verses.translation: When the mind follows wandering senses, understanding is carried off — like a boat being swept away by the wind on water.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः।

इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता।।2.68।।
verses.translation: So, Arjuna, the person whose senses are completely withdrawn from their objects is the one whose wisdom is firmly established.

verses.sanskrit:
या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी।

यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः।।2.69।।
verses.translation: What is night for all others, the self-controlled is awake in; what others are awake in, that is night for the sage who sees.

verses.sanskrit:
आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठं

समुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत्।

तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वे

स शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी।।2.70।।
verses.translation: The ocean stays full and undisturbed though rivers pour into it. Likewise, the person desires flow into but who is not disturbed by them reaches peace — not the one who chases desires.

verses.sanskrit:
विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः।

निर्ममो निरहंकारः स शांतिमधिगच्छति।।2.71।।
verses.translation: The person who gives up all desires, walks without longing, lets go of "mine" and "I" — that person reaches peace.

verses.sanskrit:
एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति।

स्थित्वाऽस्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति।।2.72।।
verses.translation: This is the state of union with the infinite (Brahman), Arjuna. Reaching it, one is no longer confused. Staying in it even at death, one attains the ultimate peace.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन।

तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव।।3.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, if you say wisdom is better than action, why are you pushing me into this terrible fight?

verses.sanskrit:
व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे।

तदेकं वद निश्िचत्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम्।।3.2।।
verses.translation: Your mixed signals are confusing me. Tell me clearly — one thing — that will lead me to the highest good.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

लोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ।

ज्ञानयोगेन सांख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम्।।3.3।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Arjuna, long ago I taught two paths in this world: the way of knowledge for the contemplative, and the way of action for the active.

verses.sanskrit:
न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते।

न च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति।।3.4।।
verses.translation: You don't become free from action by simply avoiding action, and you don't reach perfection by renouncing alone.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि कश्िचत्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत्।

कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः।।3.5।।
verses.translation: No one can stay completely inactive, not even for a moment. Nature's three qualities make everyone act, whether they want to or not.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन्।

इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते।।3.6।।
verses.translation: The person who forces their body to sit still but lets their mind dwell on sense objects is a confused hypocrite.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन।

कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते।।3.7।।
verses.translation: But the person who controls the senses with the mind and engages the body in action without attachment — that person is excellent, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः।

शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः।।3.8।।
verses.translation: Do your assigned work. Action is better than inaction. You couldn't even keep your body alive without action.

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः।

तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसंगः समाचर।।3.9।।
verses.translation: This world is bound by action — except action done as a sacred offering. So do your work as an offering, Arjuna, without attachment.

verses.sanskrit:
सहयज्ञाः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापतिः।

अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक्।।3.10।।
verses.translation: In the beginning, the Lord of beings created humans along with sacrifice and said: "By this you shall multiply. Let this fulfill your needs."

verses.sanskrit:
देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः।

परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ।।3.11।।
verses.translation: "Through this, nourish the gods, and the gods will nourish you. Supporting each other, you'll attain the highest good."

verses.sanskrit:
इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः।

तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः।।3.12।।
verses.translation: "The gods, nourished by your offerings, will give you what you need. Whoever enjoys their gifts without giving back is a thief."

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषैः।

भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात्।।3.13।।
verses.translation: Good people who eat what's left from an offering are freed from all sins. But those who cook only for themselves eat only sin.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः।

यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भवः।।3.14।।
verses.translation: Beings come from food; food comes from rain; rain comes from sacrifice; and sacrifice comes from action.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम्।

तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम्।।3.15।।
verses.translation: Action arises from the Vedic principle, which arises from the Imperishable. So the all-pervading infinite is always present in sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit:
एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः।

अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति।।3.16।।
verses.translation: Whoever doesn't follow this wheel set in motion — living in sin, indulging the senses — lives in vain, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानवः।

आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते।।3.17।।
verses.translation: But the person who finds joy in the Self, is satisfied in the Self, content in the Self alone — has nothing more to do.

verses.sanskrit:
नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन।

न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्िचदर्थव्यपाश्रयः।।3.18।।
verses.translation: Such a person gains nothing from doing and loses nothing from not doing. They depend on no being for anything.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर।

असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः।।3.19।।
verses.translation: So always do the work you ought to do, without attachment. By acting without attachment, a person reaches the highest.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादयः।

लोकसंग्रहमेवापि संपश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि।।3.20।।
verses.translation: King Janaka and others reached perfection purely through action. You too should act — if only to keep the world going.

verses.sanskrit:
यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः।

स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते।।3.21।।
verses.translation: Whatever a great person does, others follow. Whatever standard they set, the world imitates.

verses.sanskrit:
न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन।

नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि।।3.22।।
verses.translation: Arjuna, there's nothing in the three worlds I have to do, nothing I haven't already attained — yet I keep acting.

verses.sanskrit:
यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रितः।

मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः।।3.23।।
verses.translation: If I didn't tirelessly keep acting, people everywhere would follow my lead.

verses.sanskrit:
उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम्।

सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः।।3.24।।
verses.translation: If I didn't act, these worlds would fall apart. I would be the cause of chaos, destroying all creatures.

verses.sanskrit:
सक्ताः कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत।

कुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्िचकीर्षुर्लोकसंग्रहम्।।3.25।।
verses.translation: The ignorant act attached to their work, Arjuna. The wise should act just as actively — but without attachment — for the welfare of the world.

verses.sanskrit:
न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम्।

जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान् युक्तः समाचरन्।।3.26।।
verses.translation: A wise person shouldn't unsettle the ignorant who are attached to their work. Instead, acting with full engagement himself, he should inspire them to act well.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः।

अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताऽहमिति मन्यते।।3.27।।
verses.translation: Actions are carried out by nature's qualities in every way. But the person deluded by ego thinks, "I am the doer."

verses.sanskrit:
तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयोः।

गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते।।3.28।।
verses.translation: But someone who understands how the qualities and actions really work sees that qualities act on qualities — and is not attached.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढाः सज्जन्ते गुणकर्मसु।

तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत्।।3.29।।
verses.translation: People deluded by nature's qualities are attached to the actions the qualities produce. The one who knows the whole picture shouldn't upset those who don't.

verses.sanskrit:
मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा।

निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः।।3.30।।
verses.translation: Surrender all your actions to Me, with your mind fixed on the Self. Drop hopes and "mine," drop feverish excitement — and fight.

verses.sanskrit:
ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवाः।

श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभिः।।3.31।।
verses.translation: Those who follow this teaching of mine, with faith and without complaining, are freed from the bondage of action.

verses.sanskrit:
ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम्।

सर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतसः।।3.32।।
verses.translation: But those who scoff at this teaching and don't practice it — know them to be lost, without understanding in anything.

verses.sanskrit:
सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि।

प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति।।3.33।।
verses.translation: Even a wise person acts according to their own nature. Beings follow their nature. What good will suppression do?

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ।

तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ।।3.34।।
verses.translation: Attachment and aversion are built into the relationship between each sense and its object. Don't fall under their sway — they're your enemies.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्।

स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः।।3.35।।
verses.translation: Better to fail at your own duty than to succeed at someone else's. Better to die doing your own duty; another's duty is dangerous.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुषः।

अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः।।3.36।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, what actually pushes a person to act wrongly, even when they don't want to, as if something were forcing them?

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः।

महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम्।।3.37।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
It's desire. It's anger. These arise from the quality of rajas (passion). All-devouring, deeply harmful — know this to be the enemy here.

verses.sanskrit:
धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथाऽऽदर्शो मलेन च।

यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम्।।3.38।।
verses.translation: As fire is hidden by smoke, a mirror by dust, an embryo by the womb — wisdom is hidden by desire.

verses.sanskrit:
आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा।

कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च।।3.39।।
verses.translation: Arjuna, wisdom is veiled by this constant enemy of the wise — desire, which burns like an unquenchable fire.

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते।

एतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम्।।3.40।।
verses.translation: Desire hides in the senses, the mind, and the intellect. Through these it confuses the person, veiling their wisdom.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ।

पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम्।।3.41।।
verses.translation: So, Arjuna, first get your senses under control, and destroy this harmful thing that destroys wisdom and realization.

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनः।

मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धेः परतस्तु सः।।3.42।।
verses.translation: The senses are said to be higher than the body; the mind is higher than the senses; the intellect is higher than the mind; and what is higher than the intellect is the Self.

verses.sanskrit:
एवं बुद्धेः परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना।

जहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम्।।3.43।।
verses.translation: So, knowing what is higher than the intellect, steady the self with the Self. Arjuna, destroy this enemy in the form of desire — hard to defeat though it is.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

इमं विवस्वते योगं प्रोक्तवानहमव्ययम्।

विवस्वान् मनवे प्राह मनुरिक्ष्वाकवेऽब्रवीत्।।4.1।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
I taught this eternal yoga to Vivasvan, the sun god. He taught it to Manu, and Manu to Ikshvaku.

verses.sanskrit:
एवं परम्पराप्राप्तमिमं राजर्षयो विदुः।

स कालेनेह महता योगो नष्टः परन्तप।।4.2।।
verses.translation: Passed down this way, the royal sages knew it. But over a long stretch of time, that yoga was lost in this world, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
स एवायं मया तेऽद्य योगः प्रोक्तः पुरातनः।

भक्तोऽसि मे सखा चेति रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम्।।4.3।।
verses.translation: That same ancient yoga I've taught to you today, because you're my devotee and my friend. This is the supreme secret.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

अपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वतः।

कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति।।4.4।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
You were born recently; Vivasvan was born long ago. How am I to understand that you taught this at the beginning?

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

बहूनि मे व्यतीतानि जन्मानि तव चार्जुन।

तान्यहं वेद सर्वाणि न त्वं वेत्थ परन्तप।।4.5।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Many lifetimes have passed for me — and for you, Arjuna. I know them all; you don't.

verses.sanskrit:
अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन्।

प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय संभवाम्यात्ममायया।।4.6।।
verses.translation: Though I am unborn, my Self imperishable, though I am the Lord of all beings, I take my stand in my own nature and come into being through my own power.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।

अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदाऽऽत्मानं सृजाम्यहम्।।4.7।।
verses.translation: Whenever righteousness (dharma) declines and unrighteousness rises, Arjuna, I bring myself into being.

verses.sanskrit:
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्।

धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय संभवामि युगे युगे।।4.8।।
verses.translation: To protect the good, to destroy the wicked, and to re-establish dharma — I come age after age.

verses.sanskrit:
जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः।

त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन।।4.9।।
verses.translation: Anyone who truly understands my divine birth and action is not born again when they leave the body — they come to me, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
वीतरागभयक्रोधा मन्मया मामुपाश्रिताः।

बहवो ज्ञानतपसा पूता मद्भावमागताः।।4.10।।
verses.translation: Freed from attachment, fear, and anger, absorbed in me, taking refuge in me, purified by the discipline of knowledge — many have attained my state.

verses.sanskrit:
ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम्।

मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः।।4.11।।
verses.translation: Whichever way people approach me, that's how I respond to them. People follow my path, Arjuna, in all directions.

verses.sanskrit:
काङ्क्षन्तः कर्मणां सिद्धिं यजन्त इह देवताः।

क्षिप्रं हि मानुषे लोके सिद्धिर्भवति कर्मजा।।4.12।।
verses.translation: Those who seek material success offer sacrifices to the gods here, because in this world, success from action comes quickly.

verses.sanskrit:
चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः।

तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम्।।4.13।।
verses.translation: The four social orders were created by me, based on qualities and work. Though I am their maker, know that I am unchanging and don't act.

verses.sanskrit:
न मां कर्माणि लिम्पन्ति न मे कर्मफले स्पृहा।

इति मां योऽभिजानाति कर्मभिर्न स बध्यते।।4.14।।
verses.translation: Actions don't stain me, and I don't crave their results. Anyone who understands this is not bound by actions.

verses.sanskrit:
एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः।

कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वैः पूर्वतरं कृतम्।।4.15।।
verses.translation: Knowing this, the ancient seekers of liberation also performed action. So do your work, just as the ancients did long ago.

verses.sanskrit:
किं कर्म किमकर्मेति कवयोऽप्यत्र मोहिताः।

तत्ते कर्म प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात्।।4.16।।
verses.translation: What is action? What is inaction? Even the wise are confused about this. I'll tell you clearly, so that you may be freed from harm.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मणो ह्यपि बोद्धव्यं बोद्धव्यं च विकर्मणः।

अकर्मणश्च बोद्धव्यं गहना कर्मणो गतिः।।4.17।।
verses.translation: You must understand the nature of right action, of wrong action, and of inaction. The way of action is deep and hard to see.

verses.sanskrit:
कर्मण्यकर्म यः पश्येदकर्मणि च कर्म यः।

स बुद्धिमान् मनुष्येषु स युक्तः कृत्स्नकर्मकृत्।।4.18।।
verses.translation: The person who sees inaction in action and action in inaction — that person is wise, truly yoked, truly active.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्य सर्वे समारम्भाः कामसङ्कल्पवर्जिताः।

ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणं तमाहुः पण्डितं बुधाः।।4.19।।
verses.translation: The person whose every undertaking is free from desire-based plans, whose actions are burned up in the fire of wisdom — the wise call that person truly learned.

verses.sanskrit:
त्यक्त्वा कर्मफलासङ्गं नित्यतृप्तो निराश्रयः।

कर्मण्यभिप्रवृत्तोऽपि नैव किञ्चित्करोति सः।।4.20।।
verses.translation: Giving up attachment to results, always content, relying on nothing — even when doing things, such a person doesn't really do anything.

verses.sanskrit:
निराशीर्यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः।

शारीरं केवलं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम्।।4.21।।
verses.translation: Without expectation, with mind and self controlled, giving up all possessions, acting with only the body — such a person incurs no sin.

verses.sanskrit:
यदृच्छालाभसन्तुष्टो द्वन्द्वातीतो विमत्सरः।

समः सिद्धावसिद्धौ च कृत्वापि न निबध्यते।।4.22।।
verses.translation: Content with whatever comes unsought, beyond pairs of opposites, free from jealousy, even in success and failure — though they act, they are not bound.

verses.sanskrit:
गतसङ्गस्य मुक्तस्य ज्ञानावस्थितचेतसः।

यज्ञायाचरतः कर्म समग्रं प्रविलीयते।।4.23।।
verses.translation: For one who is free, whose mind is established in wisdom, who acts as an offering, all karma dissolves.

verses.sanskrit:
ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्महविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम्।

ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना।।4.24।।
verses.translation: The offering is Brahman; the sacrifice is Brahman; poured by Brahman into the fire of Brahman. Brahman alone is reached by the one who sees Brahman in all action.

verses.sanskrit:
दैवमेवापरे यज्ञं योगिनः पर्युपासते।

ब्रह्माग्नावपरे यज्ञं यज्ञेनैवोपजुह्वति।।4.25।।
verses.translation: Some yogis offer sacrifices to the gods; others offer the sacrifice itself as an offering into the fire of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रोत्रादीनीन्द्रियाण्यन्ये संयमाग्निषु जुह्वति।

शब्दादीन्विषयानन्य इन्द्रियाग्निषु जुह्वति।।4.26।।
verses.translation: Some offer the senses — hearing and the rest — into fires of self-discipline. Others offer sense objects — sound and the rest — into the fires of the senses.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वाणीन्द्रियकर्माणि प्राणकर्माणि चापरे।

आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते।।4.27।।
verses.translation: Some offer all sense-activities and life-energies into the fire of self-mastery, kindled by wisdom.

verses.sanskrit:
द्रव्ययज्ञास्तपोयज्ञा योगयज्ञास्तथापरे।

स्वाध्यायज्ञानयज्ञाश्च यतयः संशितव्रताः।।4.28।।
verses.translation: Some sacrifice with wealth, some with austerity, some with yoga, some with study and wisdom — people of strict vows.

verses.sanskrit:
अपाने जुह्वति प्राण प्राणेऽपानं तथाऽपरे।

प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणाः।।4.29।।
verses.translation: Others, practicing breath control, regulate the outflow and inflow, offering the outbreath into the inbreath and vice versa.

verses.sanskrit:
अपरे नियताहाराः प्राणान्प्राणेषु जुह्वति।

सर्वेऽप्येते यज्ञविदो यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषाः।।4.30।।
verses.translation: Others, restricting their food, offer their life-energies into their life-energies. All of these know sacrifice, and their sins are destroyed through sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजो यान्ति ब्रह्म सनातनम्।

नायं लोकोऽस्त्ययज्ञस्य कुतो़ऽन्यः कुरुसत्तम।।4.31।।
verses.translation: Those who eat the leftovers of sacrifice — the true nectar — reach eternal Brahman. This world isn't even for the non-sacrificer — how could any other world be?

verses.sanskrit:
एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे।

कर्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे।।4.32।।
verses.translation: So many kinds of sacrifices are spread out at the mouth of Brahman. Know them all as born of action; knowing this, you'll be free.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप।

सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते।।4.33।।
verses.translation: The sacrifice of wisdom is better than the sacrifice of material things, Arjuna. Every action, in its fullness, culminates in wisdom.

verses.sanskrit:
तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया।

उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः।।4.34।।
verses.translation: Seek that wisdom with humility, by asking questions, and by serving. Those who have seen the truth will teach you.

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव।

येन भूतान्यशेषेण द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि।।4.35।।
verses.translation: Having grasped this, Arjuna, you'll never fall into confusion again. You'll see every being in yourself — and then in me.

verses.sanskrit:
अपि चेदसि पापेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः पापकृत्तमः।

सर्वं ज्ञानप्लवेनैव वृजिनं सन्तरिष्यसि।।4.36।।
verses.translation: Even if you were the greatest of all sinners, you would cross over all evil on the boat of wisdom.

verses.sanskrit:
यथैधांसि समिद्धोऽग्निर्भस्मसात्कुरुतेऽर्जुन।

ज्ञानाग्निः सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुते तथा।।4.37।।
verses.translation: As a blazing fire reduces firewood to ashes, Arjuna, the fire of wisdom reduces all actions to ashes.

verses.sanskrit:
न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते।

तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति।।4.38।।
verses.translation: Nothing here purifies like wisdom. A person perfected in yoga finds it within themselves, in time.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः।

ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति।।4.39।।
verses.translation: A person with faith, committed to practice, senses under control — attains wisdom. And once attained, it soon brings supreme peace.

verses.sanskrit:
अज्ञश्चाश्रद्दधानश्च संशयात्मा विनश्यति।

नायं लोकोऽस्ति न परो न सुखं संशयात्मनः।।4.40।।
verses.translation: But the ignorant, the faithless, the doubter — they are lost. For the doubter there is no happiness here, elsewhere, or anywhere.

verses.sanskrit:
योगसंन्यस्तकर्माणं ज्ञानसंछिन्नसंशयम्।

आत्मवन्तं न कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय।।4.41।।
verses.translation: Arjuna, actions do not bind the person who has given up action through yoga, whose doubts have been cut away by wisdom, who is self-possessed.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मादज्ञानसंभूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनाऽऽत्मनः।

छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत।।4.42।।
verses.translation: So, with the sword of wisdom, cut away this doubt in your heart — born of ignorance. Take up yoga and stand up, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

संन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि।

यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्िचतम्।।5.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, you praise renouncing action — and then you praise action (yoga). Tell me clearly which one is better.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

संन्यासः कर्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ।

तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते।।5.2।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Both renunciation and the way of action lead to the highest. But of the two, the way of action is better than renouncing action.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञेयः स नित्यसंन्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्क्षति।

निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते।।5.3।।
verses.translation: Know this person as a lifelong renunciate: one who neither hates nor desires. Free from opposites, Arjuna, such a person is easily set free from bondage.

verses.sanskrit:
सांख्ययोगौ पृथग्बालाः प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिताः।

एकमप्यास्थितः सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम्।।5.4।।
verses.translation: Only children — not the wise — think of the paths of knowledge and action as different. Whoever is fully established in one gets the fruit of both.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्सांख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते।

एकं सांख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स पश्यति।।5.5।।
verses.translation: The state reached by the path of knowledge is the same state reached by the path of action. The one who sees these two as one — truly sees.

verses.sanskrit:
संन्यासस्तु महाबाहो दुःखमाप्तुमयोगतः।

योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म नचिरेणाधिगच्छति।।5.6।।
verses.translation: But renunciation without the practice of yoga is hard to attain, Arjuna. The sage committed to yoga reaches the infinite quickly.

verses.sanskrit:
योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः।

सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते।।5.7।।
verses.translation: Engaged in yoga, purified in self, with body and senses mastered, one's self becoming the Self of all beings — such a person isn't stained, even while acting.

verses.sanskrit:
नैव किंचित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित्।

पश्यन् श्रृणवन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्गच्छन्स्वपन् श्वसन्।।5.8।।
verses.translation: The person who knows the truth thinks: "I'm not really doing anything," even while seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating, walking, sleeping, breathing,

verses.sanskrit:
प्रलपन्विसृजन्गृह्णन्नुन्मिषन्निमिषन्नपि।

इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेषु वर्तन्त इति धारयन्।।5.9।।
verses.translation: speaking, releasing, grasping, opening and closing the eyes — understanding that it's the senses moving among their objects.

verses.sanskrit:
ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः।

लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा।।5.10।।
verses.translation: The person who acts without attachment, offering actions to Brahman, is not stained by sin — like a lotus leaf untouched by water.

verses.sanskrit:
कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि।

योगिनः कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वाऽऽत्मशुद्धये।।5.11।।
verses.translation: With body, mind, intellect, or even just the senses alone, yogis act without attachment, to purify themselves.

verses.sanskrit:
युक्तः कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम्।

अयुक्तः कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते।।5.12।।
verses.translation: The yogi, letting go of results, reaches lasting peace. The non-yogi, attached to results by desire, is trapped.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वकर्माणि मनसा संन्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी।

नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन्।।5.13।।
verses.translation: Mentally letting go of all actions, the self-controlled person rests peacefully in the "city of nine gates" (the body) — neither acting nor causing action.

verses.sanskrit:
न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः।

न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते।।5.14।।
verses.translation: The indwelling Self doesn't create agency, actions, or the link of action to result. It's nature (prakriti) that acts.

verses.sanskrit:
नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभुः।

अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तवः।।5.15।।
verses.translation: The all-pervading One doesn't take on anyone's sin or merit. Wisdom is hidden by ignorance, and so beings are confused.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः।

तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम्।।5.16।।
verses.translation: But for those whose ignorance is destroyed by self-knowledge, that knowledge lights up the supreme — like the sun.

verses.sanskrit:
तद्बुद्धयस्तदात्मानस्तन्निष्ठास्तत्परायणाः।

गच्छन्त्यपुनरावृत्तिं ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषाः।।5.17।।
verses.translation: With their minds on That, their selves rooted in That, with That as their goal, devoted to That — they reach a state from which they don't return, their sins wiped clean by wisdom.

verses.sanskrit:
विद्याविनयसंपन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि।

शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः।।5.18।।
verses.translation: The wise see the same reality in a learned and humble brahmin, a cow, an elephant, a dog, or even an outcaste.

verses.sanskrit:
इहैव तैर्जितः सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मनः।

निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद्ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिताः।।5.19।।
verses.translation: Those whose minds are steady in seeing sameness have conquered rebirth even here. Brahman is spotless and the same everywhere — so they are established in Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम्।

स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद्ब्रह्मणि स्थितः।।5.20।।
verses.translation: The one who knows Brahman — steady in intellect, unconfused — doesn't get excited over what is pleasant or upset by what is unpleasant.

verses.sanskrit:
बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम्।

स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते।।5.21।।
verses.translation: With the self unattached to external things, such a person finds the joy that lies in the Self. Joined in yoga with Brahman, they enjoy imperishable happiness.

verses.sanskrit:
ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते।

आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः।।5.22।।
verses.translation: Pleasures born of sense-contact are wombs of pain. They have a beginning and an end. The wise do not take their delight in them, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
शक्नोतीहैव यः सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात्।

कामक्रोधोद्भवं वेगं स युक्तः स सुखी नरः।।5.23।।
verses.translation: Whoever, even here and now, before leaving the body, can withstand the surges of desire and anger — that person is truly yoked; that person is happy.

verses.sanskrit:
योऽन्तःसुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव यः।

स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति।।5.24।।
verses.translation: The one whose happiness, delight, and light are all within — that yogi, becoming Brahman, reaches the peace of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः।

छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः।।5.25।।
verses.translation: Sages whose sins are gone, whose doubts are dispelled, whose minds are disciplined, who delight in the welfare of all beings — they reach the peace of Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
कामक्रोधवियुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम्।

अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम्।।5.26।।
verses.translation: For the disciplined ones free from desire and anger, with minds under control, knowing the Self — the peace of Brahman is close at hand, all around.

verses.sanskrit:
स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवोः।

प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ।।5.27।।
verses.translation: Shutting out external stimuli, focusing the gaze between the eyebrows, balancing the inbreath and the outbreath moving through the nostrils,

verses.sanskrit:
यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायणः।

विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो यः सदा मुक्त एव सः।।5.28।।
verses.translation: with senses, mind, and intellect under control, the sage aims at liberation, free from desire, fear, and anger — truly, always free.

verses.sanskrit:
भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम्।

सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति।।5.29।।
verses.translation: Know me as the receiver of all offerings and disciplines, the great Lord of all worlds, the friend of all beings — and you reach peace.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

अनाश्रितः कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति यः।

स संन्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रियः।।6.1।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
The person who does the work they ought to do without depending on the results — that person is the true renunciate and the true yogi; not just the one who lights no ritual fire or does no action.

verses.sanskrit:
यं संन्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव।

न ह्यसंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन।।6.2।।
verses.translation: Know that what's called renunciation is also yoga, Arjuna. No one becomes a yogi without giving up self-serving intentions.

verses.sanskrit:
आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते।

योगारूढस्य तस्यैव शमः कारणमुच्यते।।6.3।।
verses.translation: For a sage trying to climb to yoga, action is the means. For one who has reached yoga, stillness is the means.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते।

सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते।।6.4।।
verses.translation: When a person isn't attached to sense objects or to actions, having given up all self-serving intentions — then they're said to have reached yoga.

verses.sanskrit:
उद्धरेदात्मनाऽऽत्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्।

आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः।।6.5।।
verses.translation: Lift yourself by yourself. Don't let yourself fall. The Self alone is your friend; the self alone is your enemy.

verses.sanskrit:
बन्धुरात्माऽऽत्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः।

अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत्।।6.6।।
verses.translation: For the person who has conquered themselves, the Self is a friend. But to the one who hasn't, their own self acts like an enemy.

verses.sanskrit:
जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः।

शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः।।6.7।।
verses.translation: A self-controlled, peaceful person remains steady in the Supreme Self — through cold and heat, pleasure and pain, honor and dishonor.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रियः।

युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः।।6.8।।
verses.translation: The yogi who is content with knowledge and realization, unshakable, senses under control — to whom a clod of earth, a stone, and a lump of gold are alike — is truly yoked.

verses.sanskrit:
सुहृन्मित्रार्युदासीनमध्यस्थद्वेष्यबन्धुषु।

साधुष्वपि च पापेषु समबुद्धिर्विशिष्यते।।6.9।।
verses.translation: Whoever sees with equal mind well-wishers, friends, enemies, neutrals, mediators, the hateful, relatives, the righteous, and even the sinful — excels.

verses.sanskrit:
योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः।

एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रहः।।6.10।।
verses.translation: Let the yogi constantly concentrate the mind, sitting alone in a quiet place, with body and mind under control, without possessions, without cravings.

verses.sanskrit:
शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः।

नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम्।।6.11।।
verses.translation: Setting up a steady seat for oneself in a clean place, neither too high nor too low, covered with cloth, deerskin, and kusha grass, layered together,

verses.sanskrit:
तत्रैकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः।

उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये।।6.12।।
verses.translation: sitting there, making the mind one-pointed, controlling the activities of mind and senses, let the yogi practice to purify the self.

verses.sanskrit:
समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिरः।

संप्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन्।।6.13।।
verses.translation: Holding the body, head, and neck erect and still, gazing steadily at the tip of the nose, not looking around,

verses.sanskrit:
प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थितः।

मनः संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्परः।।6.14।।
verses.translation: with a serene mind, fearless, firm in a vow of celibacy, mind controlled, thoughts fixed on me — let the yogi sit, absorbed, with me as the goal.

verses.sanskrit:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदाऽऽत्मानं योगी नियतमानसः।

शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति।।6.15।।
verses.translation: Always yoking the mind this way, the yogi of disciplined mind reaches the peace abiding in me — the peace that ends in liberation.

verses.sanskrit:
नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नतः।

न चातिस्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन।।6.16।।
verses.translation: Yoga is not for someone who eats too much or eats nothing at all, not for the one who sleeps too much or stays awake too much, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु।

युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दुःखहा।।6.17।।
verses.translation: For the one whose food and recreation are moderate, whose effort in action is measured, whose sleep and waking are balanced — yoga destroys sorrow.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते।

निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा।।6.18।।
verses.translation: When the disciplined mind settles in the Self alone, free from craving for any desire — that person is said to be truly yoked.

verses.sanskrit:
यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता।

योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः।।6.19।।
verses.translation: "Like a lamp in a windless place that does not flicker" — that's the image used for a yogi whose mind is absorbed in concentration on the Self.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया।

यत्र चैवात्मनाऽऽत्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति।।6.20।।
verses.translation: In that state, where the mind becomes still through the practice of yoga, seeing the Self by the Self, one is satisfied in the Self.

verses.sanskrit:
सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद्बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम्।

वेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वतः।।6.21।।
verses.translation: In that state one knows the infinite happiness grasped by the intellect, beyond the senses — and established in it, one never wanders from the truth.

verses.sanskrit:
यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं ततः।

यस्मिन्स्थितो न दुःखेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते।।6.22।।
verses.translation: Once gained, the yogi thinks nothing greater can be gained. Established in it, they aren't shaken even by heavy sorrow.

verses.sanskrit:
तं विद्याद् दुःखसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम्।

स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा।।6.23।।
verses.translation: That disconnection from pain's hold is what should be known as yoga. It must be practiced with determination and without discouragement.

verses.sanskrit:
सङ्कल्पप्रभवान्कामांस्त्यक्त्वा सर्वानशेषतः।

मनसैवेन्द्रियग्रामं विनियम्य समन्ततः।।6.24।।
verses.translation: Completely giving up all desires born of intention, restraining the whole gang of senses with the mind from every side,

verses.sanskrit:
शनैः शनैरुपरमेद् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया।

आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत्।।6.25।।
verses.translation: let the person gradually grow still with an intellect held steady. With the mind fixed on the Self, let them think of nothing at all.

verses.sanskrit:
यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम्।

ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत्।।6.26।।
verses.translation: Wherever the restless, unsteady mind wanders off — pull it back from there, bring it under the control of the Self alone.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम्।

उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम्।।6.27।।
verses.translation: Supreme happiness comes to the yogi whose mind is completely peaceful, whose passion is stilled, who has become one with Brahman, free from stain.

verses.sanskrit:
युञ्जन्नेवं सदाऽऽत्मानं योगी विगतकल्मषः।

सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते।।6.28।।
verses.translation: Yoking the self like this constantly, the yogi, free from stain, easily reaches the infinite happiness of contact with Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि।

ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शनः।।6.29।।
verses.translation: Whose self is yoked in yoga sees the same reality everywhere — sees the Self in all beings and all beings in the Self.

verses.sanskrit:
यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति।

तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति।।6.30।।
verses.translation: Whoever sees me everywhere and sees all in me — I am not lost to them, and they are not lost to me.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थितः।

सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते।।6.31।।
verses.translation: The yogi who, established in oneness, worships me as present in all beings — however they live, they live in me.

verses.sanskrit:
आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन।

सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं सः योगी परमो मतः।।6.32।।
verses.translation: Whoever, by comparison with themselves, sees everything as equal, Arjuna — whether pleasure or pain — is considered the highest yogi.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

योऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन।

एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात् स्थितिं स्थिराम्।।6.33।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, this yoga of evenness you've described — I don't see how it can last, because the mind is so restless.

verses.sanskrit:
चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद्दृढम्।

तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम्।।6.34।।
verses.translation: The mind is really restless, Krishna — turbulent, strong, stubborn. Controlling it seems as hard as controlling the wind.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

असंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलं।

अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते।।6.35।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
No doubt about it, Arjuna — the mind is hard to control, and restless. But by practice and by detachment, it can be brought under control.

verses.sanskrit:
असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मतिः।

वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायतः।।6.36।।
verses.translation: I agree, yoga is hard to attain for one without self-control. But with effort and discipline, the one who strives can reach it.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

अयतिः श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानसः।

अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति।।6.37।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
What happens to the person with faith who strives but whose mind wanders, and who doesn't reach perfection in yoga, Krishna?

verses.sanskrit:
कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति।

अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मणः पथि।।6.38।।
verses.translation: Having fallen from both paths, don't they perish like a broken cloud — with no ground under them, deluded on the path to Brahman?

verses.sanskrit:
एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषतः।

त्वदन्यः संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते।।6.39।।
verses.translation: Krishna, please completely clear this doubt for me. There's no one else who can.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते।

नहि कल्याणकृत्कश्िचद्दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति।।6.40।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Arjuna, such a person is not destroyed — not in this world or the next. No one who does good, my friend, ever comes to grief.

verses.sanskrit:
प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वतीः समाः।

शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते।।6.41।।
verses.translation: Having reached the worlds of the righteous and lived there for countless years, the one who fell from yoga is born again in the home of the pure and the prosperous.

verses.sanskrit:
अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम्।

एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम्।।6.42।।
verses.translation: Or they may even be born into a family of wise yogis. Such a birth is very rare in this world.

verses.sanskrit:
तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम्।

यतते च ततो भूयः संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन।।6.43।।
verses.translation: There they recover the understanding from their previous life and strive again for perfection, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि सः।

जिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्तते।।6.44।।
verses.translation: By that very previous practice they're carried forward, even against their will. Just the inquiry into yoga takes a person beyond the ritual of the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रयत्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिषः।

अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम्।।6.45।।
verses.translation: But the yogi who works hard, purified of sins, perfected through many lifetimes, reaches the highest goal.

verses.sanskrit:
तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिकः।

कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्जुन।।6.46।।
verses.translation: The yogi is greater than those who practice austerity, greater than the learned, greater than ritualists. So be a yogi, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना।

श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मतः।।6.47।।
verses.translation: And among all yogis, the one who worships me with faith and inner heart absorbed in me — I consider the most deeply joined.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

मय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः।

असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु।।7.1।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Arjuna, with your mind fixed on me, taking refuge in me, practicing yoga — listen to how you can know me completely, beyond doubt.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः।

यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते।।7.2।।
verses.translation: I'll tell you fully the knowledge with realization — knowing which there's nothing else here left to know.

verses.sanskrit:
मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्िचद्यतति सिद्धये।

यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्िचन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः।।7.3।।
verses.translation: Among thousands of people, maybe one strives for perfection. Even among those who strive and attain it, maybe one truly knows me.

verses.sanskrit:
भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च।

अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा।।7.4।।
verses.translation: Earth, water, fire, air, space, mind, intellect, and ego — these are the eight divisions of my lower nature.

verses.sanskrit:
अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम्।

जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत्।।7.5।।
verses.translation: But know, Arjuna, my other, higher nature — the life-principle by which this whole universe is held up.

verses.sanskrit:
एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय।

अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा।।7.6।।
verses.translation: Understand that all beings arise from these two. I am the origin of the whole universe — and also its dissolution.

verses.sanskrit:
मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय।

मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव।।7.7।।
verses.translation: Nothing at all is higher than me, Arjuna. All this is strung on me like beads on a thread.

verses.sanskrit:
रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः।

प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु।।7.8।।
verses.translation: Arjuna, I am the taste in water, the light in the sun and moon, the sacred sound "Om" in all the Vedas, the sound in space, the strength in men.

verses.sanskrit:
पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ।

जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु।।7.9।।
verses.translation: I am the pure fragrance in earth, the brilliance in fire, the life in all beings, the austerity in those who are disciplined.

verses.sanskrit:
बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम्।

बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम्।।7.10।।
verses.translation: Know me, Arjuna, as the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intelligence of the intelligent, the splendor of the splendid.

verses.sanskrit:
बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम्।

धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ।।7.11।।
verses.translation: I am the strength of the strong — free from desire and passion. In beings I am desire that doesn't violate dharma.

verses.sanskrit:
ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये।

मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि नत्वहं तेषु ते मयि।।7.12।।
verses.translation: Whatever states of being there are — of purity (sattva), activity (rajas), and inertia (tamas) — know them as coming from me alone. But I am not in them; they are in me.

verses.sanskrit:
त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्वमिदं जगत्।

मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम्।।7.13।।
verses.translation: Confused by these three qualities, the world doesn't recognize me — the one who is beyond them, imperishable.

verses.sanskrit:
दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया।

मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते।।7.14।।
verses.translation: This divine illusion (maya) of mine, made of the three qualities, is hard to cross. Those who take refuge in me alone cross it.

verses.sanskrit:
न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढाः प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः।

माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः।।7.15।।
verses.translation: Evildoers, the confused, the lowest of men, don't take refuge in me. Their knowledge is carried off by illusion; they cling to a demonic nature.

verses.sanskrit:
चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन।

आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ।।7.16।।
verses.translation: Four kinds of good people worship me, Arjuna: the distressed, the seeker of knowledge, the seeker of success, and the wise.

verses.sanskrit:
तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्ितर्विशिष्यते।

प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रियः।।7.17।।
verses.translation: Of these, the wise one — ever steady, devoted to the One — is the best. To the wise, I am exceedingly dear, and they are dear to me.

verses.sanskrit:
उदाराः सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम्।

आस्थितः स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम्।।7.18।।
verses.translation: All of them are noble, but I hold the wise one to be my very Self. With a steady mind, they are established in me alone, the highest goal.

verses.sanskrit:
बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते।

वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः।।7.19।।
verses.translation: At the end of many lifetimes, the wise person takes refuge in me, seeing "Krishna (Vasudeva) is all this." Such a great soul is very rare.

verses.sanskrit:
कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञानाः प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः।

तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया।।7.20।।
verses.translation: Those whose wisdom is carried off by this or that desire turn to other deities, following various practices, bound by their own nature.

verses.sanskrit:
यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्तः श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति।

तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम्।।7.21।।
verses.translation: Whatever form any devotee sincerely wants to worship — I make their faith in that form steady.

verses.sanskrit:
स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते।

लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान् हि तान्।।7.22।।
verses.translation: Endowed with that faith, they seek what they want from that deity — and get the results, which are actually given by me.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम्।

देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि।।7.23।।
verses.translation: But the fruit for such people, whose understanding is limited, is limited. Worshippers of deities go to those deities. My devotees come to me.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्तं व्यक्ितमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः।

परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम्।।7.24।।
verses.translation: The unwise think of me — the unmanifest — as having just come into manifestation, not knowing my higher, imperishable, supreme nature.

verses.sanskrit:
नाहं प्रकाशः सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः।

मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम्।।7.25।।
verses.translation: Not everyone can see me clearly; I'm veiled by my yoga-maya. This confused world doesn't know me — unborn, imperishable.

verses.sanskrit:
वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन।

भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन।।7.26।।
verses.translation: I know all beings of the past, present, and future, Arjuna. But nobody knows me.

verses.sanskrit:
इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत।

सर्वभूतानि संमोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप।।7.27।।
verses.translation: Confused by the pairs of opposites arising from desire and aversion, all beings in creation are deluded from the start, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम्।

ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः।।7.28।।
