verses.translation: But those of virtuous action, whose sins have ended, freed from the delusion of opposites, worship me with firm resolve.

verses.sanskrit:
जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये।

ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम्।।7.29।।
verses.translation: Those who strive for freedom from old age and death, taking refuge in me — know Brahman completely, the Self, and all action.

verses.sanskrit:
साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदुः।

प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः।।7.30।।
verses.translation: Those who know me as the principle behind beings, behind the gods, and behind sacrifice — with minds yoked, know me even at the hour of death.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

किं तद्ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं किं कर्म पुरुषोत्तम।

अधिभूतं च किं प्रोक्तमधिदैवं किमुच्यते।।8.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
What is Brahman? What is the Self? What is action, Krishna? What is called the principle behind beings? What is the principle behind the gods?

verses.sanskrit:
अधियज्ञः कथं कोऽत्र देहेऽस्मिन्मधुसूदन।

प्रयाणकाले च कथं ज्ञेयोऽसि नियतात्मभिः।।8.2।।
verses.translation: Who is the principle behind sacrifice, and how, in this body? And how are you to be known at the hour of death by the self-controlled?

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

अक्षरं ब्रह्म परमं स्वभावोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते।

भूतभावोद्भवकरो विसर्गः कर्मसंज्ञितः।।8.3।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
The Imperishable is the supreme Brahman. Its nature is called the Self. The offering that causes beings to come into existence is called action (karma).

verses.sanskrit:
अधिभूतं क्षरो भावः पुरुषश्चाधिदैवतम्।

अधियज्ञोऽहमेवात्र देहे देहभृतां वर।।8.4।।
verses.translation: The principle behind beings is perishable nature. The principle behind the gods is the cosmic Person. The principle behind sacrifice — here in the body — is me.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम्।

यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः।।8.5।।
verses.translation: The person who, remembering me alone at the moment of death, leaves the body reaches my state of being. No doubt about it.

verses.sanskrit:
यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम्।

तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावितः।।8.6।।
verses.translation: Whatever state of being a person remembers at the last moment when leaving the body — that's the state they reach, having dwelt on it constantly.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु मामनुस्मर युध्य च।

मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्मामेवैष्यस्यसंशयम्।।8.7।।
verses.translation: So remember me always, and fight. With your mind and intellect fixed on me, you will definitely come to me.

verses.sanskrit:
अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन चेतसा नान्यगामिना।

परमं पुरुषं दिव्यं याति पार्थानुचिन्तयन्।।8.8।।
verses.translation: The person whose mind, disciplined by the practice of yoga, doesn't wander anywhere else reaches the supreme divine Person, Arjuna — by meditating on him.

verses.sanskrit:
कविं पुराणमनुशासितार

मणोरणीयांसमनुस्मरेद्यः।

सर्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूप

मादित्यवर्णं तमसः परस्तात्।।8.9।।
verses.translation: Whoever meditates on the all-knowing, the ancient, the ruler, subtler than the subtle, the sustainer of all, of unimaginable form, sun-like in color, beyond darkness —

verses.sanskrit:
प्रयाणकाले मनसाऽचलेन

भक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव।

भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक्

स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम्।।8.10।।
verses.translation: at the hour of death, with a steady mind, with devotion and the power of yoga, fully drawing the life-breath between the eyebrows — reaches the supreme divine Person.

verses.sanskrit:
यदक्षरं वेदविदो वदन्ति

विशन्ति यद्यतयो वीतरागाः।

यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति

तत्ते पदं संग्रहेण प्रवक्ष्ये।।8.11।।
verses.translation: That which the knowers of the Vedas call the Imperishable, that which the disciplined ascetics, free from passion, enter; for which they practice celibacy — I'll tell you about that goal briefly.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वद्वाराणि संयम्य मनो हृदि निरुध्य च।

मूर्ध्न्याधायात्मनः प्राणमास्थितो योगधारणाम्।।8.12।।
verses.translation: Closing all the gates of the body, confining the mind in the heart, drawing the life-breath into the head, established in yogic concentration,

verses.sanskrit:
ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म व्याहरन्मामनुस्मरन्।

यः प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं स याति परमां गतिम्।।8.13।।
verses.translation: uttering the single syllable "Om" (which is Brahman), remembering me — whoever leaves the body this way reaches the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit:
अनन्यचेताः सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः।

तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः।।8.14।।
verses.translation: The one who always remembers me, thinking of nothing else — for that ever-yoked yogi, I'm easy to reach, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म दुःखालयमशाश्वतम्।

नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मानः संसिद्धिं परमां गताः।।8.15।।
verses.translation: Great souls who have come to me, having reached the highest perfection, are not reborn in this fleeting home of suffering.

verses.sanskrit:
आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन।

मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते।।8.16।।
verses.translation: From the world of Brahma the creator on down, all worlds bring return, Arjuna. But on reaching me, there's no rebirth.

verses.sanskrit:
सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तमहर्यद्ब्रह्मणो विदुः।

रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः।।8.17।।
verses.translation: Those who know that Brahma's day lasts a thousand ages, and his night also a thousand ages — they are the ones who truly know day and night.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्ताद्व्यक्तयः सर्वाः प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे।

रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते तत्रैवाव्यक्तसंज्ञके।।8.18।।
verses.translation: At the coming of day, all manifestations come out of the unmanifest. At the coming of night, they dissolve back into that very unmanifest.

verses.sanskrit:
भूतग्रामः स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते।

रात्र्यागमेऽवशः पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे।।8.19।।
verses.translation: This same multitude of beings, coming into being again and again, dissolves helplessly at the coming of night, Arjuna, and comes forth again at the coming of day.

verses.sanskrit:
परस्तस्मात्तु भावोऽन्योऽव्यक्तोऽव्यक्तात्सनातनः।

यः स सर्वेषु भूतेषु नश्यत्सु न विनश्यति।।8.20।।
verses.translation: But beyond this unmanifest is another unmanifest — eternal being — which does not perish when all beings perish.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तस्तमाहुः परमां गतिम्।

यं प्राप्य न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम।।8.21।।
verses.translation: That unmanifest, called the Imperishable, is declared the supreme goal. Whoever reaches it does not return — that is my supreme abode.

verses.sanskrit:
पुरुषः स परः पार्थ भक्त्या लभ्यस्त्वनन्यया।

यस्यान्तःस्थानि भूतानि येन सर्वमिदं ततम्।।8.22।।
verses.translation: Arjuna, the supreme Person, in whom all beings rest and by whom all this is pervaded, is reached by unwavering devotion alone.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिमावृत्तिं चैव योगिनः।

प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ।।8.23।।
verses.translation: Now I'll tell you the times at which yogis depart, Arjuna — to return or not to return.

verses.sanskrit:
अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम्।

तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः।।8.24।।
verses.translation: Fire, light, day, the bright half of the month, the six months of the sun's northern course — departing then, the knowers of Brahman go to Brahman.

verses.sanskrit:
धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम्।

तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते।।8.25।।
verses.translation: Smoke, night, the dark half of the month, the six months of the sun's southern course — the yogi going by this path reaches the moonlight and returns.

verses.sanskrit:
शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते जगतः शाश्वते मते।

एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययाऽऽवर्तते पुनः।।8.26।।
verses.translation: These two paths of the world — the bright and the dark — are considered eternal. By one, a person reaches no-return; by the other, return.

verses.sanskrit:
नैते सृती पार्थ जानन्योगी मुह्यति कश्चन।

तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु योगयुक्तो भवार्जुन।।8.27।।
verses.translation: Knowing these two paths, Arjuna, no yogi is confused. So at all times, stay yoked in yoga, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
वेदेषु यज्ञेषु तपःसु चैव

दानेषु यत्पुण्यफलं प्रदिष्टम्।

अत्येति तत्सर्वमिदं विदित्वा

योगी परं स्थानमुपैति चाद्यम्।।8.28।।
verses.translation: Whatever merit is said to come from studying the Vedas, from sacrifices, austerities, and gifts — the yogi who knows this goes beyond all of them and reaches the supreme, original abode.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

इदं तु ते गुह्यतमं प्रवक्ष्याम्यनसूयवे।

ज्ञानं विज्ञानसहितं यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात्।।9.1।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
To you who don't criticize, I'll reveal the most profound secret — knowledge together with realization — and by knowing it you'll be free from evil.

verses.sanskrit:
राजविद्या राजगुह्यं पवित्रमिदमुत्तमम्।

प्रत्यक्षावगमं धर्म्यं सुसुखं कर्तुमव्ययम्।।9.2।।
verses.translation: This is the king of sciences, the king of secrets, the supreme purifier. It's directly realizable, in line with dharma, easy to practice, never lost.

verses.sanskrit:
अश्रद्दधानाः पुरुषा धर्मस्यास्य परन्तप।

अप्राप्य मां निवर्तन्ते मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि।।9.3।।
verses.translation: People without faith in this teaching don't reach me, Arjuna. They return to the cycle of birth and death.

verses.sanskrit:
मया ततमिदं सर्वं जगदव्यक्तमूर्तिना।

मत्स्थानि सर्वभूतानि न चाहं तेष्ववस्थितः।।9.4।।
verses.translation: All this world is pervaded by me in my unmanifest form. All beings rest in me — but I don't rest in them.

verses.sanskrit:
न च मत्स्थानि भूतानि पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम्।

भूतभृन्न च भूतस्थो ममात्मा भूतभावनः।।9.5।।
verses.translation: And yet beings don't rest in me — behold my divine power! Supporting beings but not resting in them, my Self is the source of beings.

verses.sanskrit:
यथाऽऽकाशस्थितो नित्यं वायुः सर्वत्रगो महान्।

तथा सर्वाणि भूतानि मत्स्थानीत्युपधारय।।9.6।।
verses.translation: As the mighty wind, moving everywhere, always rests in space — in the same way, all beings rest in me. Know it.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वभूतानि कौन्तेय प्रकृतिं यान्ति मामिकाम्।

कल्पक्षये पुनस्तानि कल्पादौ विसृजाम्यहम्।।9.7।।
verses.translation: All beings, Arjuna, merge into my nature at the end of a cycle. At the beginning of the next cycle, I send them forth again.

verses.sanskrit:
प्रकृतिं स्वामवष्टभ्य विसृजामि पुनः पुनः।

भूतग्राममिमं कृत्स्नमवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात्।।9.8।।
verses.translation: Taking hold of my own nature, I send forth again and again this whole multitude of beings — helpless under the force of nature.

verses.sanskrit:
न च मां तानि कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय।

उदासीनवदासीनमसक्तं तेषु कर्मसु।।9.9।।
verses.translation: But these actions don't bind me, Arjuna. I remain as if indifferent, unattached to those actions.

verses.sanskrit:
मयाऽध्यक्षेण प्रकृतिः सूयते सचराचरम्।

हेतुनाऽनेन कौन्तेय जगद्विपरिवर्तते।।9.10।।
verses.translation: Under my watch, nature produces everything that moves and everything that doesn't. That's the reason the world keeps spinning, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
अवजानन्ति मां मूढा मानुषीं तनुमाश्रितम्।

परं भावमजानन्तो मम भूतमहेश्वरम्।।9.11।।
verses.translation: Fools dismiss me when I take a human body, not knowing my higher nature as the great Lord of beings.

verses.sanskrit:
मोघाशा मोघकर्माणो मोघज्ञाना विचेतसः।

राक्षसीमासुरीं चैव प्रकृतिं मोहिनीं श्रिताः।।9.12।।
verses.translation: With futile hopes, futile actions, futile knowledge, without discrimination, they are filled with a deluding nature — demonic and deceiving.

verses.sanskrit:
महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिताः।

भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम्।।9.13।।
verses.translation: But great souls, Arjuna, partaking of the divine nature, worship me with undistracted minds, knowing me as the imperishable source of beings.

verses.sanskrit:
सततं कीर्तयन्तो मां यतन्तश्च दृढव्रताः।

नमस्यन्तश्च मां भक्त्या नित्ययुक्ता उपासते।।9.14।।
verses.translation: Always praising me, striving, firm in their vows, bowing down to me with devotion, ever-yoked, they worship me.

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानयज्ञेन चाप्यन्ये यजन्तो मामुपासते।

एकत्वेन पृथक्त्वेन बहुधा विश्वतोमुखम्।।9.15।।
verses.translation: Others, offering the sacrifice of wisdom, worship me as the One, as the distinct, as the many, facing everywhere.

verses.sanskrit:
अहं क्रतुरहं यज्ञः स्वधाऽहमहमौषधम्।

मंत्रोऽहमहमेवाज्यमहमग्निरहं हुतम्।।9.16।।
verses.translation: I am the ritual, I am the sacrifice, I am the offering to ancestors, I am the herb, I am the mantra, I am the clarified butter, I am the fire, I am the act of offering.

verses.sanskrit:
पिताऽहमस्य जगतो माता धाता पितामहः।

वेद्यं पवित्रमोंकार ऋक् साम यजुरेव च।।9.17।।
verses.translation: I am the father of this world, and the mother, the sustainer, the ancestor. I am what is to be known, the purifier, the sacred "Om," and the Rig, Sama, and Yajur Vedas.

verses.sanskrit:
गतिर्भर्ता प्रभुः साक्षी निवासः शरणं सुहृत्।

प्रभवः प्रलयः स्थानं निधानं बीजमव्ययम्।।9.18।।
verses.translation: I am the goal, the sustainer, the master, the witness, the abode, the shelter, the friend; the origin, the dissolution, the ground, the storehouse, the imperishable seed.

verses.sanskrit:
तपाम्यहमहं वर्षं निगृह्णाम्युत्सृजामि च।

अमृतं चैव मृत्युश्च सदसच्चाहमर्जुन।।9.19।।
verses.translation: I give heat; I hold back and pour out the rain. I am immortality and death, what is and what is not, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
त्रैविद्या मां सोमपाः पूतपापा

यज्ञैरिष्ट्वा स्वर्गतिं प्रार्थयन्ते।

ते पुण्यमासाद्य सुरेन्द्रलोक

मश्नन्ति दिव्यान्दिवि देवभोगान्।।9.20।।
verses.translation: The knowers of the three Vedas, the drinkers of soma, their sins cleansed, worshipping me with sacrifices, pray for the way to heaven. Reaching the pure world of Indra, they enjoy the heavenly pleasures of the gods.

verses.sanskrit:
ते तं भुक्त्वा स्वर्गलोकं विशालं

क्षीणे पुण्ये मर्त्यलोकं विशन्ति।

एव त्रयीधर्ममनुप्रपन्ना

गतागतं कामकामा लभन्ते।।9.21।।
verses.translation: Having enjoyed that vast heavenly world, when their merit runs out, they re-enter the world of mortals. That's how, following the dharma of the three Vedas, desiring pleasures, they keep coming and going.

verses.sanskrit:
अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते।

तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम्।।9.22।।
verses.translation: For those who, thinking of nothing else, worship me as ever-united, I provide what they lack and protect what they have.

verses.sanskrit:
येऽप्यन्यदेवता भक्ता यजन्ते श्रद्धयाऽन्विताः।

तेऽपि मामेव कौन्तेय यजन्त्यविधिपूर्वकम्।।9.23।।
verses.translation: Even those who, with faith, worship other deities — they too, Arjuna, are worshipping me, though not in the proper way.

verses.sanskrit:
अहं हि सर्वयज्ञानां भोक्ता च प्रभुरेव च।

न तु मामभिजानन्ति तत्त्वेनातश्च्यवन्ति ते।।9.24।।
verses.translation: Because I alone am the receiver and the Lord of all sacrifices. But they don't know me in truth, and so they fall.

verses.sanskrit:
यान्ति देवव्रता देवान् पितृ़न्यान्ति पितृव्रताः।

भूतानि यान्ति भूतेज्या यान्ति मद्याजिनोऽपि माम्।।9.25।।
verses.translation: Worshippers of deities go to those deities; ancestor-worshippers go to the ancestors; worshippers of spirits go to the spirits; and my worshippers come to me.

verses.sanskrit:
पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति।

तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः।।9.26।।
verses.translation: Whoever offers me — with devotion — a leaf, a flower, a fruit, or water, that offering of a pure-hearted devotee I accept.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत्।

यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम्।।9.27।।
verses.translation: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer, whatever you give, whatever discipline you practice — do it, Arjuna, as an offering to me.

verses.sanskrit:
शुभाशुभफलैरेवं मोक्ष्यसे कर्मबन्धनैः।

संन्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा विमुक्तो मामुपैष्यसि।।9.28।।
verses.translation: This way you'll be freed from the bondage of action and its good and bad results. With your self yoked in the yoga of renunciation, you'll come to me, free.

verses.sanskrit:
समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः।

ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम्।।9.29।।
verses.translation: I am the same in all beings. No one is hateful or dear to me. But those who worship me with devotion — they are in me, and I am in them.

verses.sanskrit:
अपि चेत्सुदुराचारो भजते मामनन्यभाक्।

साधुरेव स मन्तव्यः सम्यग्व्यवसितो हि सः।।9.30।।
verses.translation: Even the worst sinner — if they worship me with undivided devotion — should be regarded as good, because they've made the right decision.

verses.sanskrit:
क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति।

कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति।।9.31।।
verses.translation: They quickly become righteous and reach lasting peace. Know this for certain, Arjuna: my devotee is never lost.

verses.sanskrit:
मां हि पार्थ व्यपाश्रित्य येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः।

स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम्।।9.32।।
verses.translation: Even those considered of low birth, Arjuna — women, vaishyas, shudras — taking refuge in me, reach the highest goal.

verses.sanskrit:
किं पुनर्ब्राह्मणाः पुण्या भक्ता राजर्षयस्तथा।

अनित्यमसुखं लोकमिमं प्राप्य भजस्व माम्।।9.33।।
verses.translation: How much more so, then, the holy brahmins and devoted royal sages! Having entered this fleeting, unhappy world, worship me.

verses.sanskrit:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।

मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः।।9.34।।
verses.translation: Fix your mind on me, be devoted to me, offer to me, bow down to me. Fully joined to me, with me as the supreme goal, you'll come to me.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

भूय एव महाबाहो श्रृणु मे परमं वचः।

यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया।।10.1।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Listen again, Arjuna, to my supreme word, which I'll speak to you, who are dear to me, wanting your good.

verses.sanskrit:
न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः।

अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः।।10.2।।
verses.translation: Neither the hosts of gods nor the great sages know my origin. I am the source of the gods and the great sages, in every way.

verses.sanskrit:
यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम्।

असम्मूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते।।10.3।।
verses.translation: Whoever knows me as unborn, without beginning, the great Lord of the worlds — that person, not confused among mortals, is freed from all sins.

verses.sanskrit:
बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसंमोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः।

सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च।।10.4।।
verses.translation: Discernment, knowledge, freedom from confusion, patience, truth, self-control, calm, pleasure, pain, existence, non-existence, fear, fearlessness —

verses.sanskrit:
अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयशः।

भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधाः।।10.5।।
verses.translation: non-violence, equanimity, contentment, austerity, generosity, fame, infamy — these various states of beings come from me alone.

verses.sanskrit:
महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा।

मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः।।10.6।।
verses.translation: The seven great sages and the four ancient ones (Manus), sharing in my nature, were born of my mind. From them came all these creatures in the world.

verses.sanskrit:
एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः।

सोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः।।10.7।।
verses.translation: Whoever truly understands this glory and power of mine is united with me in unshakable yoga. No doubt about it.

verses.sanskrit:
अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते।

इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः।।10.8।।
verses.translation: I am the source of everything. From me, everything comes. The wise, understanding this, worship me with devotion.

verses.sanskrit:
मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम्।

कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च।।10.9।।
verses.translation: With their thoughts on me, their lives surrendered to me, telling each other about me, always speaking of me — they are content and joyful.

verses.sanskrit:
तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम्।

ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते।।10.10।।
verses.translation: To those ever-yoked devotees worshipping me with love, I give the discernment through which they come to me.

verses.sanskrit:
तेषामेवानुकम्पार्थमहमज्ञानजं तमः।

नाशयाम्यात्मभावस्थो ज्ञानदीपेन भास्वता।।10.11।।
verses.translation: Out of compassion for them, I dwell in their very self and destroy the darkness of ignorance with the bright lamp of wisdom.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

परं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान्।

पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम्।।10.12।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
You are the supreme Brahman, the supreme abode, the supreme purifier — the eternal, divine Person, primal God, unborn, all-pervading.

verses.sanskrit:
आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा।

असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे।।10.13।।
verses.translation: This is what all the sages say — and so does the divine sage Narada, and Asita, Devala, and Vyasa. And you yourself say it to me.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव।

न हि ते भगवन् व्यक्ितं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः।।10.14।।
verses.translation: I accept everything you've told me as true, Krishna. Neither gods nor demons truly understand your manifestation, Lord.

verses.sanskrit:
स्वयमेवात्मनाऽत्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम।

भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते।।10.15।।
verses.translation: You alone know yourself by yourself, supreme Person — source of beings, Lord of beings, God of gods, Lord of the world.

verses.sanskrit:
वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः।

याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि।।10.16।।
verses.translation: Please tell me, in full, your divine glories, through which you pervade all these worlds.

verses.sanskrit:
कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन्।

केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया।।10.17।।
verses.translation: How shall I know you, yogi, by constant meditation? In what various aspects should I meditate on you, Lord?

verses.sanskrit:
विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन।

भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि श्रृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम्।।10.18।।
verses.translation: Tell me again, in detail, about your yoga and your glories, Krishna. I never get enough of listening to your nectar-like words.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

हन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः।

प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे।।10.19।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Fine — I'll tell you the main ones of my divine glories, Arjuna. There's no end to the details of my manifestation.

verses.sanskrit:
अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः।

अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च।।10.20।।
verses.translation: I am the Self seated in the hearts of all beings, Arjuna. I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings.

verses.sanskrit:
आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान्।

मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी।।10.21।।
verses.translation: Of the Adityas I am Vishnu; of lights, the radiant sun. Of the Maruts (storm-gods) I am Marichi; of the stars, I am the moon.

verses.sanskrit:
वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः।

इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना।।10.22।।
verses.translation: Of the Vedas I am the Sama Veda; of the gods, I am Indra. Of the senses I am the mind; in living beings I am consciousness.

verses.sanskrit:
रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम्।

वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम्।।10.23।।
verses.translation: Of the Rudras I am Shankara (Shiva); of yakshas and rakshasas, I am Kubera. Of the Vasus I am Fire; of mountains, Meru.

verses.sanskrit:
पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम्।

सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः।।10.24।।
verses.translation: Among household priests, know me as Brihaspati — the chief. Among generals I am Skanda; among bodies of water, the ocean.

verses.sanskrit:
महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम्।

यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः।।10.25।।
verses.translation: Of the great sages I am Bhrigu; of sounds, I am the single syllable "Om." Of sacrifices I am the silent repetition of a mantra; of things unmoving, the Himalayas.

verses.sanskrit:
अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः।

गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः।।10.26।।
verses.translation: Of all trees I am the ashvattha (sacred fig); of divine sages, Narada; of celestial musicians, Chitraratha; of perfected beings, Kapila.

verses.sanskrit:
उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम्।

ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम्।।10.27।।
verses.translation: Among horses know me as Ucchaishravas, born from the churning for nectar; among royal elephants, Airavata; among humans, the king.

verses.sanskrit:
आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक्।

प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः।।10.28।।
verses.translation: Among weapons I am the thunderbolt; among cows, the wish-fulfilling Kamadhenu. I am the god of love in those who reproduce; among serpents, I am Vasuki.

verses.sanskrit:
अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम्।

पितृ़णामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम्।।10.29।।
verses.translation: Of the nagas I am Ananta; of water-beings, Varuna. Of ancestors, Aryaman; among rulers, I am Yama (god of death).

verses.sanskrit:
प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम्।

मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम्।।10.30।।
verses.translation: Of the demons I am Prahlada; of counters, I am Time. Of animals, the lion; of birds, Garuda.

verses.sanskrit:
पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम्।

झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी।।10.31।।
verses.translation: Of purifiers I am the wind; of warriors, Rama. Of fish I am the makara; of rivers, the Ganga.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन।

अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम्।।10.32।।
verses.translation: Of creations I am the beginning, the end, and the middle, Arjuna. Of sciences I am the science of the Self; among debaters, I am the debate itself.

verses.sanskrit:
अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च।

अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताऽहं विश्वतोमुखः।।10.33।।
verses.translation: Of letters I am "A"; of grammatical compounds, the dual compound. I alone am imperishable Time; I am the Creator facing all directions.

verses.sanskrit:
मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम्।

कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा।।10.34।।
verses.translation: I am all-devouring death, and the source of what is yet to come. Of feminine qualities: fame, fortune, speech, memory, intelligence, steadiness, forgiveness.

verses.sanskrit:
बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम्।

मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकरः।।10.35।।
verses.translation: Among hymns I am the Brihat-saman; of meters, the Gayatri. Of months I am Margashirsha (late autumn); of seasons, flower-bearing spring.

verses.sanskrit:
द्यूतं छलयतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम्।

जयोऽस्मि व्यवसायोऽस्मि सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम्।।10.36।।
verses.translation: I am the gambling of cheats, the splendor of the splendid. I am victory, I am determination, I am the goodness of the good.

verses.sanskrit:
वृष्णीनां वासुदेवोऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनंजयः।

मुनीनामप्यहं व्यासः कवीनामुशना कविः।।10.37।।
verses.translation: Of the Vrishnis I am Krishna (Vasudeva); of the Pandavas, Arjuna. Of sages I am Vyasa; among poets, Ushanas.

verses.sanskrit:
दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम्।

मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम्।।10.38।।
verses.translation: Among those who punish, I am the rod; among those who seek victory, I am statecraft. Of secrets I am silence; I am the wisdom of the wise.

verses.sanskrit:
यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन।

न तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम्।।10.39।।
verses.translation: Whatever is the seed of any being — that is me, Arjuna. No being, moving or unmoving, exists without me.

verses.sanskrit:
नान्तोऽस्ति मम दिव्यानां विभूतीनां परंतप।

एष तूद्देशतः प्रोक्तो विभूतेर्विस्तरो मया।।10.40।।
verses.translation: There's no end to my divine glories, Arjuna. What I've said is just a brief sample of the extent of my magnificence.

verses.sanskrit:
यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा।

तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसंभवम्।।10.41।।
verses.translation: Whatever is glorious, prosperous, or mighty — know that every such thing has sprung from a fragment of my splendor.

verses.sanskrit:
अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन।

विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत्।।10.42।।
verses.translation: But what's the use of knowing all this in detail, Arjuna? I stand holding up this entire universe with a single fragment of myself.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Vision of the Cosmic Form

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

मदनुग्रहाय परमं गुह्यमध्यात्मसंज्ञितम्।

यत्त्वयोक्तं वचस्तेन मोहोऽयं विगतो मम।।11.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
As a favor to me, you've taught me the supreme secret known as the Self. By those words, my confusion is gone.

verses.sanskrit:
भवाप्ययौ हि भूतानां श्रुतौ विस्तरशो मया।

त्वत्तः कमलपत्राक्ष माहात्म्यमपि चाव्ययम्।।11.2।।
verses.translation: Lotus-eyed one, I've heard from you in detail about the origin and dissolution of beings, and about your imperishable majesty.

verses.sanskrit:
एवमेतद्यथात्थ त्वमात्मानं परमेश्वर।

द्रष्टुमिच्छामि ते रूपमैश्वरं पुरुषोत्तम।।11.3।।
verses.translation: That's just how it is, supreme Lord, just as you've described yourself. I want to see your lordly form, supreme Person.

verses.sanskrit:
मन्यसे यदि तच्छक्यं मया द्रष्टुमिति प्रभो।

योगेश्वर ततो मे त्वं दर्शयाऽत्मानमव्ययम्।।11.4।।
verses.translation: If you think it's possible for me to see, Lord, then, O Lord of yoga, show me your imperishable Self.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

पश्य मे पार्थ रूपाणि शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः।

नानाविधानि दिव्यानि नानावर्णाकृतीनि च।।11.5।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Arjuna, behold my forms — by hundreds and thousands, of different kinds, divine, of many colors and shapes.

verses.sanskrit:
पश्यादित्यान्वसून्रुद्रानश्िवनौ मरुतस्तथा।

बहून्यदृष्टपूर्वाणि पश्याऽश्चर्याणि भारत।।11.6।।
verses.translation: Behold the Adityas, Vasus, Rudras, the two Ashvins, and the Maruts. Behold many wonders never seen before, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
इहैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं पश्याद्य सचराचरम्।

मम देहे गुडाकेश यच्चान्यद्द्रष्टुमिच्छसि।।11.7।।
verses.translation: Right here today, see the whole universe — moving and unmoving — gathered into one in my body, Arjuna, and anything else you want to see.

verses.sanskrit:
न तु मां शक्यसे द्रष्टुमनेनैव स्वचक्षुषा।

दिव्यं ददामि ते चक्षुः पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम्।।11.8।।
verses.translation: But you can't see me with your own eye. I'll give you a divine eye — behold my lordly power of yoga!

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

एवमुक्त्वा ततो राजन्महायोगेश्वरो हरिः।

दर्शयामास पार्थाय परमं रूपमैश्वरम्।।11.9।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Having said this, O king, the great Lord of yoga, Hari (Krishna), revealed to Arjuna his supreme lordly form —

verses.sanskrit:
अनेकवक्त्रनयनमनेकाद्भुतदर्शनम्।

अनेकदिव्याभरणं दिव्यानेकोद्यतायुधम्।।11.10।।
verses.translation: with many mouths and eyes, with many wondrous sights, with many divine ornaments, with many divine weapons raised;

verses.sanskrit:
दिव्यमाल्याम्बरधरं दिव्यगन्धानुलेपनम्।

सर्वाश्चर्यमयं देवमनन्तं विश्वतोमुखम्।।11.11।।
verses.translation: wearing divine garlands and robes, anointed with divine perfumes — all-wonderful, radiant, infinite, with faces everywhere.

verses.sanskrit:
दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता।

यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः।।11.12।।
verses.translation: If the splendor of a thousand suns were to blaze forth all at once in the sky, it might resemble the splendor of that Great Soul.

verses.sanskrit:
तत्रैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं प्रविभक्तमनेकधा।

अपश्यद्देवदेवस्य शरीरे पाण्डवस्तदा।।11.13।।
verses.translation: There Arjuna saw the whole universe, in all its divisions, gathered together in the body of that God of gods.

verses.sanskrit:
ततः स विस्मयाविष्टो हृष्टरोमा धनञ्जयः।

प्रणम्य शिरसा देवं कृताञ्जलिरभाषत।।11.14।।
verses.translation: Then, stunned with amazement, hair standing on end, Arjuna, bowing his head to the Lord, spoke with hands folded:

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

पश्यामि देवांस्तव देव देहे

सर्वांस्तथा भूतविशेषसङ्घान्।

ब्रह्माणमीशं कमलासनस्थ

मृषींश्च सर्वानुरगांश्च दिव्यान्।।11.15।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
God, in your body I see all the gods and the hosts of all beings — Brahma the creator on his lotus, all the sages, and the divine serpents.

verses.sanskrit:
अनेकबाहूदरवक्त्रनेत्रं

पश्यामि त्वां सर्वतोऽनन्तरूपम्।

नान्तं न मध्यं न पुनस्तवादिं

पश्यामि विश्वेश्वर विश्वरूप।।11.16।।
verses.translation: I see you with countless arms, bellies, mouths, and eyes — form without end, everywhere. I see neither your end, nor middle, nor beginning, Lord of the universe, universal form.

verses.sanskrit:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रिणं च

तेजोराशिं सर्वतोदीप्तिमन्तम्।

पश्यामि त्वां दुर्निरीक्ष्यं समन्ता

द्दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिमप्रमेयम्।।11.17।।
verses.translation: I see you with crown, club, and discus — a mass of brilliance blazing everywhere, hard to look at on every side, with the brilliance of blazing fire and sun, beyond measure.

verses.sanskrit:
त्वमक्षरं परमं वेदितव्यं

त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम्।

त्वमव्ययः शाश्वतधर्मगोप्ता

सनातनस्त्वं पुरुषो मतो मे।।11.18।।
verses.translation: You are the Imperishable, the supreme to be known; you are the final resting place of this universe. You are the eternal guardian of dharma; you, I know, are the primal Person.

verses.sanskrit:
अनादिमध्यान्तमनन्तवीर्य

मनन्तबाहुं शशिसूर्यनेत्रम्।

पश्यामि त्वां दीप्तहुताशवक्त्रम्

स्वतेजसा विश्वमिदं तपन्तम्।।11.19।।
verses.translation: Without beginning, middle, or end; of infinite power; of countless arms; with the sun and moon as your eyes, a blazing fire as your mouth, scorching this universe with your radiance — I see you.

verses.sanskrit:
द्यावापृथिव्योरिदमन्तरं हि

व्याप्तं त्वयैकेन दिशश्च सर्वाः।

दृष्ट्वाऽद्भुतं रूपमुग्रं तवेदं

लोकत्रयं प्रव्यथितं महात्मन्।।11.20।।
verses.translation: The space between sky and earth, and all the directions, are filled by you alone. Seeing this wondrous, terrifying form of yours, the three worlds tremble, Great Soul.

verses.sanskrit:
अमी हि त्वां सुरसङ्घाः विशन्ति

केचिद्भीताः प्राञ्जलयो गृणन्ति।

स्वस्तीत्युक्त्वा महर्षिसिद्धसङ्घाः

स्तुवन्ति त्वां स्तुतिभिः पुष्कलाभिः।।11.21।।
verses.translation: The hosts of gods enter you. Some, afraid, praise you with folded hands. The great sages and perfected beings cry "may it be well!" and sing your glory in abundant hymns.

verses.sanskrit:
रुद्रादित्या वसवो ये च साध्या

विश्वेऽश्िवनौ मरुतश्चोष्मपाश्च।

गन्धर्वयक्षासुरसिद्धसङ्घा

वीक्षन्ते त्वां विस्मिताश्चैव सर्वे।।11.22।।
verses.translation: The Rudras, Adityas, Vasus, Sadhyas, Vishvedevas, the two Ashvins, the Maruts, the ancestors, the hosts of gandharvas, yakshas, demons, and perfected beings — they all gaze at you in wonder.

verses.sanskrit:
रूपं महत्ते बहुवक्त्रनेत्रं

महाबाहो बहुबाहूरुपादम्।

बहूदरं बहुदंष्ट्राकरालं

दृष्ट्वा लोकाः प्रव्यथितास्तथाऽहम्।।11.23।।
verses.translation: Seeing your great form, with many mouths and eyes, Arjuna, with many arms, thighs, feet, many bellies, many fearsome tusks — the worlds tremble, and so do I.

verses.sanskrit:
नभःस्पृशं दीप्तमनेकवर्णं

व्यात्ताननं दीप्तविशालनेत्रम्।

दृष्ट्वा हि त्वां प्रव्यथितान्तरात्मा

धृतिं न विन्दामि शमं च विष्णो।।11.24।।
verses.translation: Seeing you touching the sky, blazing with many colors, mouths wide, great flaming eyes — my inmost self trembles; I find no firmness or peace, Vishnu.

verses.sanskrit:
दंष्ट्राकरालानि च ते मुखानि

दृष्ट्वैव कालानलसन्निभानि।

दिशो न जाने न लभे च शर्म

प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास।।11.25।।
verses.translation: Seeing your mouths, terrible with tusks, like the fires of cosmic dissolution, I lose my bearings and find no refuge. Be gracious, Lord of gods, home of the worlds.

verses.sanskrit:
अमी च त्वां धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राः

सर्वे सहैवावनिपालसङ्घैः।

भीष्मो द्रोणः सूतपुत्रस्तथाऽसौ

सहास्मदीयैरपि योधमुख्यैः।।11.26।।
verses.translation: Into you rush all the sons of Dhritarashtra, along with the hosts of kings — Bhishma, Drona, even Karna, and our chief warriors too —

verses.sanskrit:
वक्त्राणि ते त्वरमाणा विशन्ति

दंष्ट्राकरालानि भयानकानि।

केचिद्विलग्ना दशनान्तरेषु

संदृश्यन्ते चूर्णितैरुत्तमाङ्गैः।।11.27।।
verses.translation: hurrying into your mouths, terrifying with tusks, horrifying to behold. Some, caught between the tusks, are seen with their heads ground to powder.

verses.sanskrit:
यथा नदीनां बहवोऽम्बुवेगाः

समुद्रमेवाभिमुखाः द्रवन्ति।

तथा तवामी नरलोकवीरा

विशन्ति वक्त्राण्यभिविज्वलन्ति।।11.28।।
verses.translation: As many torrents of rivers rush toward the ocean, so do these heroes of the world of men rush into your flaming mouths.

verses.sanskrit:
यथा प्रदीप्तं ज्वलनं पतङ्गा

विशन्ति नाशाय समृद्धवेगाः।

तथैव नाशाय विशन्ति लोका

स्तवापि वक्त्राणि समृद्धवेगाः।।11.29।।
verses.translation: As moths rush headlong into a blazing fire to their destruction, so these creatures rush into your mouths to their destruction.

verses.sanskrit:
लेलिह्यसे ग्रसमानः समन्ता

ल्लोकान्समग्रान्वदनैर्ज्वलद्भिः।

तेजोभिरापूर्य जगत्समग्रं

भासस्तवोग्राः प्रतपन्ति विष्णो।।11.30।।
verses.translation: Swallowing all the worlds on every side with your flaming mouths, you lick them up. Your fierce rays, filling the whole universe with brilliance, burn, Vishnu.

verses.sanskrit:
आख्याहि मे को भवानुग्ररूपो

नमोऽस्तु ते देववर प्रसीद।

विज्ञातुमिच्छामि भवन्तमाद्यं

न हि प्रजानामि तव प्रवृत्तिम्।।11.31।।
verses.translation: Tell me who you are, fearsome one — I bow to you, best of gods; be gracious. I want to understand you, the primal one, because I can't comprehend your purpose.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

कालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत्प्रवृद्धो

लोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः।

ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वे

येऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः।।11.32।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
I am Time, the mighty destroyer of the worlds, set out to destroy the worlds. Even without you, none of these warriors arrayed in the opposing armies will live.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्व

जित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम्।

मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेव

निमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन्।।11.33।।
verses.translation: So stand up; win fame. Defeat your enemies and enjoy a prosperous kingdom. I have already killed these. You just be the outer cause, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
द्रोणं च भीष्मं च जयद्रथं च

कर्णं तथाऽन्यानपि योधवीरान्।

मया हतांस्त्वं जहि मा व्यथिष्ठा

युध्यस्व जेतासि रणे सपत्नान्।।11.34।।
verses.translation: Drona, Bhishma, Jayadratha, Karna, and other warrior-heroes — I have killed them. Don't hesitate. Fight — you'll defeat your enemies in battle.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

एतच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं केशवस्य

कृताञ्जलिर्वेपमानः किरीटी।

नमस्कृत्वा भूय एवाह कृष्णं

सगद्गदं भीतभीतः प्रणम्य।।11.35।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
On hearing those words of Krishna, Arjuna, trembling, folded his hands and bowed. Prostrating himself, overcome with fear, he spoke to Krishna again, stammering:

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

स्थाने हृषीकेश तव प्रकीर्त्या

जगत् प्रहृष्यत्यनुरज्यते च।

रक्षांसि भीतानि दिशो द्रवन्ति

सर्वे नमस्यन्ति च सिद्धसङ्घाः।।11.36।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Rightly, Krishna, the world delights and rejoices in your praise. The demons flee in fear in all directions, and all the perfected beings bow to you.

verses.sanskrit:
कस्माच्च ते न नमेरन्महात्मन्

गरीयसे ब्रह्मणोऽप्यादिकर्त्रे।

अनन्त देवेश जगन्निवास

त्वमक्षरं सदसत्तत्परं यत्।।11.37।।
verses.translation: And why wouldn't they bow to you, Great Soul — greater than even Brahma the first creator? Infinite Lord of gods, refuge of the worlds — you are the Imperishable, being and non-being, and what is beyond.

verses.sanskrit:
त्वमादिदेवः पुरुषः पुराण

स्त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम्।

वेत्तासि वेद्यं च परं च धाम

त्वया ततं विश्वमनन्तरूप।।11.38।।
verses.translation: You are the primal God, the ancient Person. You are the supreme resting place of this universe. You are the knower and the known, the highest goal. By you, this universe is pervaded, infinite-formed one.

verses.sanskrit:
वायुर्यमोऽग्निर्वरुणः शशाङ्कः

प्रजापतिस्त्वं प्रपितामहश्च।

नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु सहस्रकृत्वः

पुनश्च भूयोऽपि नमो नमस्ते।।11.39।।
verses.translation: You are Vayu (the wind), Yama (death), Agni (fire), Varuna (water), the Moon, Prajapati (creator), and the great ancestor. A thousand salutations to you — again and again, salutations, salutations!

verses.sanskrit:
नमः पुरस्तादथ पृष्ठतस्ते

नमोऽस्तु ते सर्वत एव सर्व।

अनन्तवीर्यामितविक्रमस्त्वं

सर्वं समाप्नोषि ततोऽसि सर्वः।।11.40।।
verses.translation: Salutations to you from in front and from behind; salutations from every side, O all. Infinite in valor, immeasurable in might, you pervade everything — so you are everything.

verses.sanskrit:
सखेति मत्वा प्रसभं यदुक्तं

हे कृष्ण हे यादव हे सखेति।

अजानता महिमानं तवेदं

मया प्रमादात्प्रणयेन वापि।।11.41।।
verses.translation: Whatever I said rashly, in carelessness or in love — calling you "Krishna," "Yadava," "friend," thinking of you as a friend, not knowing your greatness —

verses.sanskrit:
यच्चावहासार्थमसत्कृतोऽसि

विहारशय्यासनभोजनेषु।

एकोऽथवाप्यच्युत तत्समक्षं

तत्क्षामये त्वामहमप्रमेयम्।।11.42।।
verses.translation: and whatever disrespect I showed you, in fun — playing, resting, sitting, eating, alone or in front of others — I beg you, Unshaken One, to forgive me.

verses.sanskrit:
पितासि लोकस्य चराचरस्य

त्वमस्य पूज्यश्च गुरुर्गरीयान्।

न त्वत्समोऽस्त्यभ्यधिकः कुतोऽन्यो

लोकत्रयेऽप्यप्रतिमप्रभाव।।11.43।।
verses.translation: You are the father of the world — moving and unmoving — its object of worship, its most venerable teacher. There is no one equal to you — how could anyone be greater? — in the three worlds, O unmatched Power.

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मात्प्रणम्य प्रणिधाय कायं

प्रसादये त्वामहमीशमीड्यम्।

पितेव पुत्रस्य सखेव सख्युः

प्रियः प्रियायार्हसि देव सोढुम्।।11.44।।
verses.translation: So I prostrate myself, bowing my body, begging your grace, adorable Lord. As father to son, as friend to friend, as lover to beloved — bear with me, God.

verses.sanskrit:
अदृष्टपूर्वं हृषितोऽस्मि दृष्ट्वा

भयेन च प्रव्यथितं मनो मे।

तदेव मे दर्शय देव रूपं

प्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास।।11.45।।
verses.translation: Seeing what was never seen before, I am full of joy — but my mind shakes with fear. Show me, God, that other form. Be gracious, Lord of gods, home of the worlds.

verses.sanskrit:
किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रहस्त

मिच्छामि त्वां द्रष्टुमहं तथैव।

तेनैव रूपेण चतुर्भुजेन

सहस्रबाहो भव विश्वमूर्ते।।11.46।।
verses.translation: I want to see you as before — with crown, club, and discus in hand. Take on that four-armed form, thousand-armed one, universal form.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

मया प्रसन्नेन तवार्जुनेदं

रूपं परं दर्शितमात्मयोगात्।

तेजोमयं विश्वमनन्तमाद्यं

यन्मे त्वदन्येन न दृष्टपूर्वम्।।11.47।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
By my grace, Arjuna, this supreme form has been shown to you, by my own yogic power — luminous, universal, infinite, primal — seen by no one but you.

verses.sanskrit:
न वेदयज्ञाध्ययनैर्न दानै

र्न च क्रियाभिर्न तपोभिरुग्रैः।

एवंरूपः शक्य अहं नृलोके

द्रष्टुं त्वदन्येन कुरुप्रवीर।।11.48।।
verses.translation: Not by study of the Vedas, nor by sacrifices, nor by gifts, nor by rituals, nor by severe austerities, can I be seen in such a form in the human world by anyone other than you, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
मा ते व्यथा मा च विमूढभावो

दृष्ट्वा रूपं घोरमीदृङ्ममेदम्।

व्यपेतभीः प्रीतमनाः पुनस्त्वं

तदेव मे रूपमिदं प्रपश्य।।11.49।।
verses.translation: Don't be afraid or bewildered by seeing this fearsome form of mine. Fearless, with a happy heart, behold once again this other form of mine.

verses.sanskrit:
सञ्जय उवाच

इत्यर्जुनं वासुदेवस्तथोक्त्वा

स्वकं रूपं दर्शयामास भूयः।

आश्वासयामास च भीतमेनं

भूत्वा पुनः सौम्यवपुर्महात्मा।।11.50।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Having said this, Krishna showed Arjuna his own form once again; and the Great Soul, resuming his gentle form, comforted the frightened Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

दृष्ट्वेदं मानुषं रूपं तवसौम्यं जनार्दन।

इदानीमस्मि संवृत्तः सचेताः प्रकृतिं गतः।।11.51।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Seeing this gentle human form of yours, Krishna, my thoughts are calm. I'm back to my natural self.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

सुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम।

देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः।।11.52।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
This form of mine that you saw is very hard to see. Even the gods are always eager to behold this form.

verses.sanskrit:
नाहं वेदैर्न तपसा न दानेन न चेज्यया।

शक्य एवंविधो द्रष्टुं दृष्टवानसि मां यथा।।11.53।।
verses.translation: Not by the Vedas, not by austerities, not by gifts, not by sacrifice, can I be seen in the form you've seen me in.

verses.sanskrit:
भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्यमहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन।

ज्ञातुं दृष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परंतप।।11.54।।
verses.translation: But by undivided devotion, Arjuna, I can be known like this, truly seen, and even entered.

verses.sanskrit:
मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः।

निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव।।11.55।।
verses.translation: The one who works for me, holds me as the supreme goal, is devoted to me, free from attachment, free from hostility toward any being — comes to me, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचएवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते।येचाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः।।12.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Of those who constantly worship you as you are, and those who worship the imperishable unmanifest — which of them knows yoga better?

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचमय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते।श्रद्धया परयोपेतास्ते मे युक्ततमा मताः।।12.2।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Those who fix their minds on me, always yoked, worship me with supreme faith — I consider them the most deeply joined.

verses.sanskrit: ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते।सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यं च कूटस्थमचलं ध्रुवम्।।12.3।।
verses.translation: But those who worship the Imperishable — the indefinable, unmanifest, omnipresent, unthinkable, unchanging, immovable, eternal —

verses.sanskrit: संनियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धयः।ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रताः।।12.4।।
verses.translation: controlling the senses, even-minded toward everything, delighting in the welfare of all beings — they too reach me.

verses.sanskrit:
क्लेशोऽधिकतरस्तेषामव्यक्तासक्तचेतसाम्।

अव्यक्ता हि गतिर्दुःखं देहवद्भिरवाप्यते।।12.5।।
verses.translation: But the path is harder for those focused on the unmanifest. The unmanifest goal is very difficult for embodied beings to reach.

verses.sanskrit: ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्पराः।अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते।।12.6।।
verses.translation: But those who surrender all their actions to me, with me as their goal, worship me, meditating on me with undivided yoga —

verses.sanskrit: तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात्।भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम्।।12.7।।
verses.translation: for those whose minds have entered into me, I quickly become the rescuer from the ocean of death and rebirth, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय।निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः।।12.8।।
verses.translation: Fix your mind on me alone; let your intellect rest in me. Then, without doubt, you'll live in me alone.

verses.sanskrit: अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम्।अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय।।12.9।।
verses.translation: But if you can't steadily fix your mind on me, then try to reach me through the practice of yoga, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव।मदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन् सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि।।12.10।।
verses.translation: If you can't even practice, devote yourself to working for me. Just performing actions for my sake, you'll reach perfection.

verses.sanskrit: अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः।सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान्।।12.11।।
verses.translation: If you can't do even that, taking refuge in yoga toward me, give up the fruits of all your actions — with self-control.

verses.sanskrit: श्रेयो हि ज्ञानमभ्यासाज्ज्ञानाद्ध्यानं विशिष्यते।ध्यानात्कर्मफलत्यागस्त्यागाच्छान्तिरनन्तरम्।।12.12।।
verses.translation: Knowledge is better than practice; meditation is better than knowledge; renouncing the fruits of action is better than meditation; from that renunciation, peace follows right away.

verses.sanskrit: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च।निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी।।12.13।।
verses.translation: The one who hates no creature, who is friendly and compassionate to all, free from "mine" and "I," even-minded in pleasure and pain, forgiving;

verses.sanskrit: सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः।मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः।।12.14।।
verses.translation: always content, yoked, self-controlled, firm in resolve, with mind and intellect offered to me — that devotee is dear to me.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः।हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः।।12.15।।
verses.translation: The one who doesn't disturb the world, and isn't disturbed by the world, free from delight, anger, fear, and agitation — is dear to me.

verses.sanskrit: अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः।सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः।।12.16।।
verses.translation: The one who wants nothing, is pure, skilled, detached, untroubled, giving up all undertakings — that devotee is dear to me.

verses.sanskrit: यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति।शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्ितमान्यः स मे प्रियः।।12.17।।
verses.translation: The one who neither rejoices nor hates, neither grieves nor desires, renouncing both good and evil, full of devotion — is dear to me.

verses.sanskrit: समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः।शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः।।12.18।।
verses.translation: The same toward enemy and friend, the same in honor and dishonor, the same in cold and heat, in pleasure and pain, free from attachment;

verses.sanskrit: तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येनकेनचित्।अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्ितमान्मे प्रियो नरः।।12.19।।
verses.translation: to whom blame and praise are the same, silent, content with anything, homeless, steady in mind, full of devotion — that person is dear to me.

verses.sanskrit: ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते।श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रियाः।।12.20।।
verses.translation: But those who follow this nectar of dharma that I've described, with faith, with me as the supreme goal — those devotees are extremely dear to me.

chapters.chapter_title_english: The Body and the One Who Knows It

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च।

एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव।।13.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, I want to know about nature (prakriti) and Person (purusha), the field (the body) and the knower of the field, knowledge and what is to be known.

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचइदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते।एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः।।13.2।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Arjuna, this body is called the "field." The one who knows this field, those who see clearly call the "knower of the field."

verses.sanskrit:
क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत।

क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम।।13.3।।
verses.translation: Know me also as the knower of the field in every field. Knowledge of the field and its knower — that's what I consider true knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक् च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत्।स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श्रृणु।।13.4।।
verses.translation: What the field is, what its nature, its changes, where it comes from, who the knower is and what his powers — hear this briefly from me.

verses.sanskrit: ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक्।ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्िचतैः।।13.5।।
verses.translation: This has been sung by the sages in many ways — in distinct hymns, and in the well-reasoned, decisive verses of the Brahma-sutras.

verses.sanskrit: महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च।इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः।।13.6।।
verses.translation: The five great elements, the ego, the intellect, the unmanifest, the ten senses and the one (mind), the five fields of the senses;

verses.sanskrit: इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतनाधृतिः।एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम्।।13.7।।
verses.translation: desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, the body, intelligence, steadiness — this is a brief description of the field with its changes.

verses.sanskrit: अमानित्वमदम्भित्वमहिंसा क्षान्तिरार्जवम्।आचार्योपासनं शौचं स्थैर्यमात्मविनिग्रहः।।13.8।।
verses.translation: Humility, honesty, non-violence, patience, uprightness, service to the teacher, purity, steadiness, self-control;

verses.sanskrit: इन्द्रियार्थेषु वैराग्यमनहङ्कार एव च।जन्ममृत्युजराव्याधिदुःखदोषानुदर्शनम्।।13.9।।
verses.translation: lack of interest in sense objects, and absence of ego; insight into the suffering of birth, death, old age, and sickness;

verses.sanskrit: असक्ितरनभिष्वङ्गः पुत्रदारगृहादिषु।नित्यं च समचित्तत्वमिष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु।।13.10।।
verses.translation: non-attachment, not identifying with son, wife, home, and so on; and constant evenness of mind in both desirable and undesirable events;

verses.sanskrit: मयि चानन्ययोगेन भक्ितरव्यभिचारिणी।विविक्तदेशसेवित्वमरतिर्जनसंसदि।।13.11।।
verses.translation: and unswerving devotion to me through undivided yoga; going to solitary places, distaste for crowds of people;

verses.sanskrit: अध्यात्मज्ञाननित्यत्वं तत्त्वज्ञानार्थदर्शनम्।एतज्ज्ञानमिति प्रोक्तमज्ञानं यदतोन्यथा।।13.12।।
verses.translation: steadiness in self-knowledge, and insight into the goal of true knowledge — this is what is called knowledge; anything opposed to it is ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वाऽमृतमश्नुते।अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते।।13.13।।
verses.translation: I'll describe what should be known — knowing which, one attains immortality: the beginningless supreme Brahman, which is said to be neither being nor non-being.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम्।सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति।।13.14।।
verses.translation: It has hands and feet everywhere, eyes, heads, and faces everywhere, ears on all sides — it stands enveloping everything in the world.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम्।असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च।।13.15।।
verses.translation: Appearing to have the qualities of all the senses, yet free from all senses; unattached, yet supporting all; free from the qualities, yet the experiencer of the qualities.

verses.sanskrit: बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च।सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत्।।13.16।।
verses.translation: Inside and outside all beings; unmoving and also moving; because it is so subtle, it can't be grasped; it is far away — and yet near.

verses.sanskrit: अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम्।भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च।।13.17।।
verses.translation: Undivided, yet it seems divided among beings. It should be known as the sustainer of beings — their devourer and their creator.

verses.sanskrit: ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते।ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम्।।13.18।।
verses.translation: The Light of lights, it is said to be beyond darkness. It is knowledge, the knowable, the goal of knowledge — seated in the heart of all.

verses.sanskrit: इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः।मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते।।13.19।।
verses.translation: So the field, knowledge, and what is to be known have been briefly described. My devotee, understanding this, attains my state of being.

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि।विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसंभवान्।।13.20।।
verses.translation: Know both nature (prakriti) and Person (purusha) to be without beginning. Know also that changes and the qualities are born of nature.

verses.sanskrit: कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते।पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते।।13.21।।
verses.translation: Nature is said to be the cause in the production of the body and its activities. Person is said to be the cause in the experience of pleasure and pain.

verses.sanskrit: पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान्।कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु।।13.22।।
verses.translation: For Person, seated in nature, experiences the qualities born of nature. Attachment to the qualities is the cause of birth in good and bad wombs.

verses.sanskrit: उपद्रष्टाऽनुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः।परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः।।13.23।।
verses.translation: The supreme Person in this body is also called the spectator, the permitter, the sustainer, the experiencer, the great Lord, the supreme Self.

verses.sanskrit: य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैःसह।सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते।।13.24।।
verses.translation: Whoever understands Person and nature with its qualities in this way — however they are living — is not born again.

verses.sanskrit: ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना।अन्ये सांख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे।।13.25।।
verses.translation: Some see the Self in themselves by themselves through meditation; others by the yoga of analysis (Sankhya), and others by the yoga of action.

verses.sanskrit: अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वाऽन्येभ्य उपासते।तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः।।13.26।।
verses.translation: Yet others, not knowing, worship as they have heard from others. They too cross beyond death, sticking to what they have heard.

verses.sanskrit: यावत्सञ्जायते किञ्चित्सत्त्वं स्थावरजङ्गमम्।क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञसंयोगात्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ।।13.27।।
verses.translation: Arjuna, whatever being is born — moving or unmoving — know that it arises from the union of the field and the knower of the field.

verses.sanskrit: समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम्।विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति।।13.28।।
verses.translation: Whoever sees the supreme Lord dwelling equally in all beings, imperishable among the perishing — truly sees.

verses.sanskrit: समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम्।न हिनस्त्यात्मनाऽऽत्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम्।।13.29।।
verses.translation: For seeing the same Lord established everywhere, one doesn't destroy oneself by oneself — and so reaches the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit: प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वशः।यः पश्यति तथाऽऽत्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति।।13.30।।
verses.translation: The one who sees that all action is done by nature alone, and that the Self is therefore not the doer — truly sees.

verses.sanskrit: यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति।तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा।।13.31।।
verses.translation: When one sees the diverse states of beings as resting in the One, and their expansion as coming from that alone — then one attains Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः।शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते।।13.32।।
verses.translation: Being without beginning and without qualities, the imperishable supreme Self, Arjuna, though dwelling in the body, neither acts nor is stained.

verses.sanskrit: यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते।सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथाऽऽत्मा नोपलिप्यते।।13.33।।
verses.translation: As all-pervading space, because of its subtlety, isn't stained, the Self, seated everywhere in the body, isn't stained either.

verses.sanskrit: यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः।क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत।।13.34।।
verses.translation: As the one sun lights up this whole world, the knower of the field (the Self) lights up the entire field, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा।भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम्।।13.35।।
verses.translation: Those who, with the eye of knowledge, perceive the distinction between field and knower, and the freedom of beings from nature — they reach the Supreme.

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचपरं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम्।यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः।।14.1।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Again I'll tell you the supreme knowledge, the best of all knowledge, by knowing which the sages have gone from this world to supreme perfection.

verses.sanskrit: इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः।सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च।।14.2।।
verses.translation: Resorting to this knowledge, having become like me, they are not born at creation or troubled at dissolution.

verses.sanskrit: मम योनिर्महद्ब्रह्म तस्मिन् गर्भं दधाम्यहम्।संभवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत।।14.3।।
verses.translation: My womb is the great Brahman (nature); in it I plant the seed. From that, Arjuna, all beings are born.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तयः सम्भवन्ति याः।तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता।।14.4।।
verses.translation: Whatever forms come out of any womb, Arjuna — the great Brahman is their womb, and I am the seed-giving father.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसंभवाः।निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम्।।14.5।।
verses.translation: The three qualities — sattva (purity), rajas (passion), and tamas (dullness) — born of nature, tie the imperishable embodied one down to the body, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम्।सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ।।14.6।।
verses.translation: Among these, sattva, because of its purity, is illuminating and healthy. It binds by attachment to happiness and attachment to knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम्।तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम्।।14.7।।
verses.translation: Know that rajas is passion, the source of thirst and attachment. It binds the embodied, Arjuna, through attachment to action.

verses.sanskrit: तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम्।प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत।।14.8।।
verses.translation: Know that tamas, born of ignorance, confuses all embodied beings. It binds through carelessness, laziness, and sleep.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्मणि भारत।ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत।।14.9।।
verses.translation: Sattva attaches a person to happiness, rajas to action, Arjuna. But tamas, veiling knowledge, attaches to heedlessness.

verses.sanskrit: रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत।रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा।।14.10।।
verses.translation: Sattva rises by overcoming rajas and tamas; rajas rises by overcoming sattva and tamas; and tamas rises by overcoming sattva and rajas.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते।ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत।।14.11।।
verses.translation: When the light of knowledge streams out through all the gates of this body, know that sattva is dominant.

verses.sanskrit: लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्मणामशमः स्पृहा।रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ।।14.12।।
verses.translation: Greed, activity, undertaking of actions, restlessness, craving — these arise when rajas is dominant, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च।तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन।।14.13।।
verses.translation: Darkness, inactivity, heedlessness, and confusion — these arise when tamas is dominant, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत्।तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते।।14.14।।
verses.translation: If the embodied one dies while sattva prevails, they reach the pure worlds of those who know the highest.

verses.sanskrit: रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते।तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते।।14.15।।
verses.translation: Dying in rajas, one is reborn among those attached to action. Dying in tamas, one is reborn in the wombs of the deluded.

verses.sanskrit: कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम्।रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम्।।14.16।।
verses.translation: The fruit of good action is said to be pure — sattvic. The fruit of rajas is pain; the fruit of tamas is ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च।प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च।।14.17।।
verses.translation: From sattva comes knowledge; from rajas, greed; from tamas, heedlessness, confusion, and ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः।जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसाः।।14.18।।
verses.translation: Those established in sattva rise upward; those in rajas stay in the middle; those in tamas, settled in the lowest quality, sink down.

verses.sanskrit: नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति।गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति।।14.19।।
verses.translation: When one sees no agent other than the three qualities and knows that which is higher than the qualities — one attains my state of being.

verses.sanskrit: गुणानेतानतीत्य त्रीन्देही देहसमुद्भवान्।जन्ममृत्युजरादुःखैर्विमुक्तोऽमृतमश्नुते।।14.20।।
verses.translation: Transcending these three qualities which are the source of the body, the embodied one, freed from birth, death, old age, and pain, attains immortality.

verses.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचकैर्लिंगैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो।किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते।।14.21।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
What are the signs, Lord, by which one who has transcended these three qualities is known? How does that person behave? How do they go beyond the qualities?

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचप्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव।न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति।।14.22।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Arjuna, the one who neither hates illumination, activity, or confusion when they arise, nor longs for them when they are absent;

verses.sanskrit: उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते।गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येव योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते।।14.23।।
verses.translation: who sits as if indifferent, not disturbed by the qualities; who thinks "the qualities are doing their work" and stands firm, unmoved;

verses.sanskrit: समदुःखसुखः स्वस्थः समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः।तुल्यप्रियाप्रियो धीरस्तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुतिः।।14.24।।
verses.translation: the same in pleasure and pain, self-contained, treating a clod, stone, and gold the same; treating the liked and disliked the same, steady, the same in praise and blame of himself;

verses.sanskrit: मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः।सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते।।14.25।।
verses.translation: the same in honor and dishonor, the same toward friend and foe, giving up all undertakings — that person has gone beyond the qualities.

verses.sanskrit: मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्ितयोगेन सेवते।स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान् ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते।।14.26।।
verses.translation: Whoever serves me with the unswerving yoga of devotion — going beyond the qualities, becomes fit to become Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाऽहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च।शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च।।14.27।।
verses.translation: For I am the foundation of Brahman — immortal, imperishable, of eternal dharma, and of absolute happiness.

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम्।छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित्।।15.1।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
They speak of an imperishable sacred fig tree (ashvattha), with roots above and branches below. Its leaves are the Vedic hymns. Whoever knows this tree knows the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा     गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः।अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि     कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके।।15.2।।
verses.translation: Below and above its branches spread, fed by the three qualities of nature; its twigs are the objects of sense. Its roots, also spreading below, give rise to action in the human world.

verses.sanskrit: न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते    नान्तो न चादिर्न च संप्रतिष्ठा।अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूल    मसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा।।15.3।।
verses.translation: The tree's true form isn't seen here — not its end, its beginning, or its foundation. Cutting down this firmly-rooted tree with the strong axe of non-attachment,

verses.sanskrit: ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्य    यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः।तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये    यतः प्रवृत्तिः प्रसृता पुराणी।।15.4।।
verses.translation: one should seek that place, from which, once reached, no one returns: "I take refuge in that primal Person, from whom the ancient stream of activity has flowed."

verses.sanskrit: निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा    अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः।द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञै    र्गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत्।।15.5।।
verses.translation: Free from pride and delusion, having conquered the evil of attachment, always abiding in the Self, desires withdrawn, freed from the pairs of opposites called pleasure and pain — the unconfused reach that imperishable abode.

verses.sanskrit: न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः।यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम।।15.6।।
verses.translation: Neither the sun, nor the moon, nor fire lights up that place. Having reached it, no one returns — that is my supreme abode.

verses.sanskrit: ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः।मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति।।15.7।।
verses.translation: An eternal part of me, becoming a living being (jiva) in the world of the living, draws to itself the mind and the five senses that rest in nature.

verses.sanskrit: शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः।गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात्।।15.8।।
verses.translation: When the Lord of the body takes up a body and when he leaves it, he takes these along and moves on — as the wind carries scents from their sources.

verses.sanskrit: श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च।अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते।।15.9।।
verses.translation: Presiding over ear, eye, touch, taste, smell — and the mind — he experiences the objects of sense.

verses.sanskrit: उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम्।विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुषः।।15.10।।
verses.translation: Whether leaving or staying, or experiencing, joined with the qualities — the confused don't see him; but those with the eye of knowledge see.

verses.sanskrit: यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम्।यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः।।15.11।।
verses.translation: Striving yogis also see him seated in themselves. But the unintelligent, with unrefined selves, though striving, don't see him.

verses.sanskrit: यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम्।यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम्।।15.12।।
verses.translation: The radiance of the sun that lights up the whole world, and the radiance in the moon and fire — know that radiance to be mine.

verses.sanskrit: गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा।पुष्णामि चौषधीः सर्वाः सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मकः।।15.13।।
verses.translation: Entering the earth, I sustain all beings by my power. Becoming the nourishing moon, I feed all plants.

verses.sanskrit: अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः।प्राणापानसमायुक्तः पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम्।।15.14।।
verses.translation: Becoming the digestive fire in the body of breathing beings, joined with the inward and outward breaths, I digest the fourfold food.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो    मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च।वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो    वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम्।।15.15।।
verses.translation: And I am seated in the hearts of all. From me come memory, knowledge, and their loss. I alone am to be known through all the Vedas; I am the author of Vedanta and the knower of the Vedas.

verses.sanskrit: द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च।क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते।।15.16।।
verses.translation: There are two persons (purushas) in the world — the perishable and the imperishable. The perishable is all beings. The imperishable is called the unchanging.

verses.sanskrit: उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः।यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः।।15.17।।
verses.translation: But the supreme Person is another — called the supreme Self, the imperishable Lord who enters the three worlds and sustains them.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तमः।अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः।।15.18।।
verses.translation: Since I transcend the perishable and am higher even than the imperishable, I am known in the world and in the Veda as Purushottama — the Supreme Person.

verses.sanskrit: यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम्।स सर्वविद्भजति मां सर्वभावेन भारत।।15.19।।
verses.translation: The one who, without confusion, knows me as Purushottama knows everything — and worships me with their whole being, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयाऽनघ।एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत।।15.20।।
verses.translation: So, Arjuna, this most secret teaching has been given by me. Having understood it, one becomes wise, and has done all that needs to be done.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

अभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिः ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः।

दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम्।।16.1।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
Fearlessness, purity of heart, steadfastness in the yoga of knowledge, generosity, self-control, sacrifice, study of scripture, discipline, honesty;

verses.sanskrit: अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम्।दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम्।।16.2।।
verses.translation: non-violence, truthfulness, absence of anger, renunciation, calm, not speaking ill of others, compassion for beings, freedom from greed, gentleness, modesty, steadiness;

verses.sanskrit:
तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता।

भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत।।16.3।।
verses.translation: vigor, forgiveness, strength, purity, freedom from hatred, absence of arrogance — these are the marks of a person born with divine qualities, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च।अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम्।।16.4।।
verses.translation: Showiness, arrogance, self-importance, anger, harshness, and ignorance — these, Arjuna, belong to a person born with demonic qualities.

verses.sanskrit: दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता।मा शुचः सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव।।16.5।।
verses.translation: Divine qualities lead to liberation; demonic qualities lead to bondage. Don't grieve, Arjuna — you were born with divine qualities.

verses.sanskrit: द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन् दैव आसुर एव च।दैवो विस्तरशः प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे श्रृणु।।16.6।।
verses.translation: Two kinds of beings exist in this world: the divine and the demonic. I've described the divine at length. Now listen as I describe the demonic, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुराः।न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते।।16.7।।
verses.translation: Demonic people don't know what they should do or what they should not do. Purity, good conduct, truth — none of these is in them.

verses.sanskrit: असत्यमप्रतिष्ठं ते जगदाहुरनीश्वरम्।अपरस्परसम्भूतं किमन्यत्कामहैतुकम्।।16.8।।
verses.translation: "The world has no truth," they say, "no moral basis, no God. It is produced by the union of male and female, driven by lust — what else?"

verses.sanskrit: एतां दृष्टिमवष्टभ्य नष्टात्मानोऽल्पबुद्धयः।प्रभवन्त्युग्रकर्माणः क्षयाय जगतोऽहिताः।।16.9।।
verses.translation: Holding such views, these lost souls — of small understanding and cruel deeds — arise as enemies of the world, aiming at its destruction.

verses.sanskrit: काममाश्रित्य दुष्पूरं दम्भमानमदान्विताः।मोहाद्गृहीत्वासद्ग्राहान्प्रवर्तन्तेऽशुचिव्रताः।।16.10।।
verses.translation: Clinging to insatiable lust, full of hypocrisy, pride, and arrogance, holding corrupt ideas out of confusion, they act with impure resolve.

verses.sanskrit: चिन्तामपरिमेयां च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिताः।कामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्िचताः।।16.11।।
verses.translation: Consumed by endless worries ending only at death, making the satisfaction of desires their supreme goal, convinced that this is all there is —

verses.sanskrit: आशापाशशतैर्बद्धाः कामक्रोधपरायणाः।ईहन्ते कामभोगार्थमन्यायेनार्थसञ्चयान्।।16.12।।
verses.translation: bound by hundreds of chains of expectation, given over to lust and anger, they strive to pile up wealth by unjust means for the gratification of their desires.

verses.sanskrit: इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम्।इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम्।।16.13।।
verses.translation: "This I've got today; this desire I'll fulfill. This is mine; and this wealth will also be mine."

verses.sanskrit: असौ मया हतः शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि।ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी।।16.14।।
verses.translation: "That enemy I've killed; and others too I'll kill. I'm the lord; I'm the enjoyer; I'm successful, powerful, and happy."

verses.sanskrit: आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया।यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिताः।।16.15।।
verses.translation: "I'm rich and well-born. Who is equal to me? I'll sacrifice, I'll give, I'll enjoy." So, deluded by ignorance,

verses.sanskrit: अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृताः।प्रसक्ताः कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरकेऽशुचौ।।16.16।।
verses.translation: scattered by many anxieties, caught in the net of delusion, clinging to the gratification of desires — they fall into a foul hell.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मसम्भाविताः स्तब्धा धनमानमदान्विताः।यजन्ते नामयज्ञैस्ते दम्भेनाविधिपूर्वकम्।।16.17।।
verses.translation: Self-important, stubborn, drunk with wealth and pride, they perform sacrifices in name only, for show, not according to the proper way.

verses.sanskrit: अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिताः।मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयकाः।।16.18।।
verses.translation: Given to egotism, force, insolence, lust, and anger, these spiteful people hate me — seated in their own bodies and in others.

verses.sanskrit: तानहं द्विषतः क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान्।क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु।।16.19।।
verses.translation: These hateful, cruel evildoers, the lowest of men, I repeatedly hurl into demonic wombs in the cycle of rebirths.

verses.sanskrit: असुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि।मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम्।।16.20।।
verses.translation: Entering demonic wombs, the confused, not attaining me from birth to birth, fall from there to an even lower state, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः।कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत्।।16.21।।
verses.translation: Threefold is the gateway to hell, destructive of the self: lust, anger, and greed. So a person should let go of these three.

verses.sanskrit: एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः।आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम्।।16.22।।
verses.translation: The person freed from these three gates of darkness, Arjuna, works for their own good — and by that reaches the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit: यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारतः।न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम्।।16.23।।
verses.translation: Whoever casts aside the guidance of scripture and acts under the impulse of desire reaches neither perfection, nor happiness, nor the supreme goal.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्याकार्यव्यवस्थितौ।ज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म कर्तुमिहार्हसि।।16.24।।
verses.translation: So let scripture be your authority for deciding what to do and what not to do. Knowing what scripture prescribes, you should act here in this world.

verses.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयाऽन्विताः।तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः।।17.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, what about those who set aside scriptural rules but still worship with faith? Is their state one of sattva, rajas, or tamas?

verses.sanskrit: श्री भगवानुवाचत्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा।सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श्रृणु।।17.2।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
The faith of embodied beings is of three kinds, born of their own nature — sattvic, rajasic, and tamasic. Listen.

verses.sanskrit: सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत।श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः।।17.3।।
verses.translation: Each person's faith matches their nature, Arjuna. A person is shaped by their faith: as their faith is, so are they.

verses.sanskrit: यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः।प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः।।17.4।।
verses.translation: Sattvic people worship the gods; rajasic, the yakshas and demons; tamasic worship ghosts and spirits.

verses.sanskrit: अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः।दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः।।17.5।।
verses.translation: Those who practice severe austerities not prescribed by scripture, bound by hypocrisy and ego, driven by the force of desire and passion,

verses.sanskrit: कर्षयन्तः शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः।मां चैवान्तःशरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान्।।17.6।।
verses.translation: senseless, torturing the group of elements in the body — and me, dwelling within the body — know them to have demonic resolve.

verses.sanskrit: आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः।यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं श्रृणु।।17.7।।
