verses.translation: The food that each person enjoys is also of three kinds — and so are sacrifice, austerity, and gifts. Hear the distinction.

verses.sanskrit: आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः।रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः।।17.8।।
verses.translation: Foods that promote long life, vitality, strength, health, happiness, and cheer — savory, substantial, satisfying — are dear to sattvic people.

verses.sanskrit: कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिनः।आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः।।17.9।।
verses.translation: Foods that are bitter, sour, salty, very hot, pungent, dry, and burning — causing pain, misery, and sickness — are dear to rajasic people.

verses.sanskrit: यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत्।उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम्।।17.10।।
verses.translation: Food that is stale, flavorless, rotten, decayed, leftover, and impure is dear to tamasic people.

verses.sanskrit: अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते।यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः।।17.11।।
verses.translation: The sacrifice offered according to proper rules by those not seeking its fruits, convinced "this must be done" — that is sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: अभिसंधाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत्।इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम्।।17.12।।
verses.translation: But the sacrifice offered aiming for results, or for show, Arjuna — know that to be rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम्।श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते।।17.13।।
verses.translation: A sacrifice done contrary to the rules — no food distributed, no sacred verses, no gifts, and no faith — is called tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम्।ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते।।17.14।।
verses.translation: Worshipping the gods, the twice-born, teachers, and the wise; purity, uprightness, celibacy, non-violence — these are called the austerity of the body.

verses.sanskrit: अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत्।स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते।।17.15।।
verses.translation: Speech that causes no pain, truthful, pleasant and beneficial, and regular recitation of the scriptures — these are the austerity of speech.

verses.sanskrit: मनःप्रसादः सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रहः।भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते।।17.16।।
verses.translation: Serenity of mind, gentleness, silence, self-control, and purity of disposition — this is called the austerity of the mind.

verses.sanskrit: श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्ित्रविधं नरैः।अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते।।17.17।।
verses.translation: This threefold austerity, practiced with supreme faith by people who are yoked and aren't seeking results — is called sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत्।क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम्।।17.18।।
verses.translation: Austerity performed with showiness for the sake of respect, honor, and reverence is called rajasic — unstable and fleeting.

verses.sanskrit: मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः।परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्।।17.19।।
verses.translation: Austerity practiced out of foolish notions, with self-torture, or aimed at ruining someone else — is called tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे।देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम्।।17.20।।
verses.translation: A gift given with the thought "it ought to be given," to someone who can't repay, at the proper place, time, and to a worthy person — that gift is considered sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः।दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम्।।17.21।।
verses.translation: A gift given grudgingly, or with the expectation of return, or aiming at the result — is considered rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते।असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्।।17.22।।
verses.translation: A gift given in the wrong place, at the wrong time, to the unworthy, without respect, with contempt — is called tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: तत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः।ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा।।17.23।।
verses.translation: "Om Tat Sat" — this is said to be the threefold designation of Brahman. By these, long ago, the brahmins, the Vedas, and the sacrifices were established.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः।प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम्।।17.24।।
verses.translation: So, beginning with "Om," acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity as prescribed in scripture are always begun by those who know Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः।दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षि।।17.25।।
verses.translation: With the word "Tat," without aiming at results, the various acts of sacrifice, austerity, and gift are performed by those seeking liberation.

verses.sanskrit: सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते।प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते।।17.26।।
verses.translation: The word "Sat" is used in the sense of reality and in the sense of goodness; and for an auspicious act too, Arjuna, the word "Sat" is used.

verses.sanskrit: यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते।कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते।।17.27।।
verses.translation: Steadiness in sacrifice, austerity, and gift is also called "Sat"; and action for the sake of That is indeed called "Sat."

verses.sanskrit: अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत्।असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह।।17.28।।
verses.translation: Whatever is sacrificed, given, or performed — and whatever austerity is done — without faith is called "asat," Arjuna. It is nothing here and nothing in the hereafter.

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच

संन्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वमिच्छामि वेदितुम्।

त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केशिनिषूदन।।18.1।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
Krishna, I want to understand the truth of renunciation (sannyasa) — and also of letting go of action (tyaga) — each separately.

verses.sanskrit:
श्री भगवानुवाच

काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं संन्यासं कवयो विदुः।

सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः।।18.2।।
verses.translation:
Krishna said:
The sages understand renunciation as giving up desire-driven actions; the wise call tyaga the letting go of the fruits of all actions.

verses.sanskrit:
त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म प्राहुर्मनीषिणः।

यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे।।18.3।।
verses.translation: Some thinkers say action should be abandoned because it's flawed; others say acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity should not be given up.

verses.sanskrit: निश्चयं श्रृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम।त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविधः संप्रकीर्तितः।।18.4।।
verses.translation: Hear my conclusion on this matter of tyaga, Arjuna. Tyaga has been declared to be of three kinds.

verses.sanskrit: यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत्।यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम्।।18.5।।
verses.translation: Acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity should not be abandoned — they should be done. Sacrifice, gift, and austerity purify the wise.

verses.sanskrit: एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च।कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्िचतं मतमुत्तमम्।।18.6।।
verses.translation: But even these actions should be done while letting go of attachment and fruits — Arjuna, this is my final, decisive opinion.

verses.sanskrit: नियतस्य तु संन्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते।मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः।।18.7।।
verses.translation: Giving up an obligatory action is not proper. Giving it up out of confusion is called tamasic renunciation.

verses.sanskrit: दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म कायक्लेशभयात्त्यजेत्।स कृत्वा राजसं त्यागं नैव त्यागफलं लभेत्।।18.8।।
verses.translation: Whoever abandons action because it's painful — afraid of bodily trouble — performs rajasic renunciation; they won't get its real fruit.

verses.sanskrit: कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन।सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः।।18.9।।
verses.translation: But when an obligatory action is done, Arjuna, while letting go of attachment and fruit, because it simply ought to be done — that letting go is considered sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म कुशले नानुषज्जते।त्यागी सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः।।18.10।।
verses.translation: The sattvic renunciate, of steady intellect and cut-through doubts, neither hates unpleasant action nor is attached to pleasant action.

verses.sanskrit: न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः।यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते।।18.11।।
verses.translation: An embodied being can't give up actions completely. But whoever lets go of the fruits of action — that person is called the renouncer.

verses.sanskrit: अनिष्टमिष्टं मिश्रं च त्रिविधं कर्मणः फलम्।भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य न तु संन्यासिनां क्वचित्।।18.12।।
verses.translation: Undesired, desired, and mixed — threefold is the fruit of action that comes after death to those who haven't let go. But for those who have renounced, there's no such fruit.

verses.sanskrit: पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे।सांख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्वकर्मणाम्।।18.13।।
verses.translation: Listen to these five causes for the accomplishment of all actions, Arjuna, as declared in the conclusions of Sankhya:

verses.sanskrit: अधिष्ठानं तथा कर्ता करणं च पृथग्विधम्।विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टा दैवं चैवात्र पञ्चमम्।।18.14।।
verses.translation: the body, the agent, the various instruments, the various and distinct activities, and divine providence as the fifth.

verses.sanskrit: शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म प्रारभते नरः।न्याय्यं वा विपरीतं वा पञ्चैते तस्य हेतवः।।18.15।।
verses.translation: Whatever action a person undertakes with body, speech, or mind — right or wrong — these five are its causes.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रैवं सति कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः।पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः।।18.16।।
verses.translation: Given that, whoever — with an untrained understanding — sees only the pure Self as the agent is dull-witted and does not see.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य नाहंकृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते।हत्वापि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते।।18.17।।
verses.translation: Whose state is free from ego, whose intellect is unstained — though they kill these people, they don't really kill, and aren't bound.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना।करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसंग्रहः।।18.18।।
verses.translation: The knower, the object of knowledge, and the knowledge itself — these three move action. The instrument, the action, and the agent — these three make up action.

verses.sanskrit: ज्ञानं कर्म च कर्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः।प्रोच्यते गुणसंख्याने यथावच्छृणु तान्यपि।।18.19।।
verses.translation: In Sankhya teaching, knowledge, action, and agent are each said to be of three kinds, according to the three qualities of nature. Hear about these too.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते।अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम्।।18.20।।
verses.translation: The knowledge by which one sees in all beings the one, imperishable Being — undivided in the divided — know that knowledge to be sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान्।वेत्ति सर्वेषु भूतेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम्।।18.21।।
verses.translation: The knowledge that sees in all beings various distinct entities of different kinds — know that to be rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: यत्तु कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्ये सक्तमहैतुकम्।अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पं च तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्।।18.22।।
verses.translation: But the knowledge which clings to one effect as though it were everything, without reason, without basis in truth, and trivial — is called tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: नियतं सङ्गरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम्।अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते।।18.23।।
verses.translation: An obligatory action done without attachment, without desire or hatred, by one who seeks no fruit — is called sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: यत्तु कामेप्सुना कर्म साहङ्कारेण वा पुनः।क्रियते बहुलायासं तद्राजसमुदाहृतम्।।18.24।।
verses.translation: But the action done with great effort by one who seeks results, or impelled by ego — is called rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम्।मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते।।18.25।।
verses.translation: An action begun out of confusion, without regard for consequences, loss, injury, or ability — is called tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: मुक्तसङ्गोऽनहंवादी धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः।सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योर्निर्विकारः कर्ता सात्त्विक उच्यते।।18.26।।
verses.translation: Free from attachment, not talking about "me," endowed with firmness and energy, unmoved by success or failure — that agent is called sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: रागी कर्मफलप्रेप्सुर्लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः।हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्ता राजसः परिकीर्तितः।।18.27।।
verses.translation: Passionate, desiring the fruit of action, greedy, naturally harmful, impure, full of joy and sorrow — that agent is called rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: अयुक्तः प्राकृतः स्तब्धः शठो नैष्कृतिकोऽलसः।विषादी दीर्घसूत्री च कर्ता तामस उच्यते।।18.28।।
verses.translation: Unyoked, vulgar, stubborn, deceitful, malicious, lazy, depressed, and procrastinating — that agent is called tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं श्रृणु।प्रोच्यमानमशेषेण पृथक्त्वेन धनञ्जय।।18.29।।
verses.translation: Now listen, Arjuna, to the threefold division of intellect and steadiness according to the qualities — I'll tell you fully and separately.

verses.sanskrit: प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च कार्याकार्ये भयाभये।बन्धं मोक्षं च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी।।18.30।।
verses.translation: The intellect that knows action and restraint from action, what should be done and what shouldn't, fear and fearlessness, bondage and liberation, Arjuna — that is sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: यया धर्ममधर्मं च कार्यं चाकार्यमेव च।अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ राजसी।।18.31।।
verses.translation: The intellect that doesn't rightly understand what is dharma and what is not, what should be done and what shouldn't — that, Arjuna, is rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: अधर्मं धर्ममिति या मन्यते तमसाऽऽवृता।सर्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी।।18.32।।
verses.translation: The intellect that, enveloped in darkness, takes unrighteousness for righteousness and sees everything the wrong way — that is tamasic, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: धृत्या यया धारयते मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः।योगेनाव्यभिचारिण्या धृतिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी।।18.33।।
verses.translation: The unwavering steadiness with which, through yoga, a person controls the functions of the mind, life-breaths, and senses — that steadiness is sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: यया तु धर्मकामार्थान् धृत्या धारयतेऽर्जुन।प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी।।18.34।।
verses.translation: But the steadiness by which one holds on to dharma, pleasure, and wealth, Arjuna, with attachment and craving for fruits — that steadiness is rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च।न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी।।18.35।।
verses.translation: The steadiness with which a foolish person doesn't give up sleep, fear, grief, depression, and conceit — that steadiness, Arjuna, is tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं श्रृणु मे भरतर्षभ।अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तं च निगच्छति।।18.36।।
verses.translation: Now hear from me, Arjuna, the three kinds of happiness that come after long practice and bring the end of pain.

verses.sanskrit: यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम्।तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम्।।18.37।।
verses.translation: The happiness that seems like poison at first but turns into nectar in the end, born of the clear insight of the Self — is called sattvic.

verses.sanskrit: विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम्।परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम्।।18.38।।
verses.translation: The happiness that arises from sense contact — like nectar at first, poison in the end — is considered rajasic.

verses.sanskrit: यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः।निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम्।।18.39।।
verses.translation: Happiness that confuses the self from beginning to end, coming from sleep, laziness, and heedlessness — is called tamasic.

verses.sanskrit: न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः।सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्ित्रभिर्गुणैः।।18.40।।
verses.translation: There is no being on earth or even in heaven among the gods who is free from these three qualities born of nature.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परंतप।कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः।।18.41।।
verses.translation: The duties of brahmins, warriors, merchants, and laborers, Arjuna, are distributed according to the qualities born of their own nature.

verses.sanskrit: शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च।ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म स्वभावजम्।।18.42।।
verses.translation: Calm, self-control, austerity, purity, patience, uprightness, knowledge, realization, and faith in the spiritual — these are the natural duties of the brahmin.

verses.sanskrit: शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम्।दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म स्वभावजम्।।18.43।।
verses.translation: Valor, vigor, firmness, skill, not running from battle, generosity, and a leader's nature — these are the natural duties of the warrior.

verses.sanskrit: कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम्।परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम्।।18.44।।
verses.translation: Farming, cattle-rearing, and trade are the natural duties of the merchant. Work consisting of service is the natural duty of the laborer.

verses.sanskrit: स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः।स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु।।18.45।।
verses.translation: A person reaches perfection by being engaged in their own duty. Hear how one engaged in their own duty reaches perfection.

verses.sanskrit: यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम्।स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः।।18.46।।
verses.translation: By worshipping, through his own work, the One from whom the activity of all beings flows and by whom all this is pervaded — a person reaches perfection.

verses.sanskrit: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्।स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम्।।18.47।।
verses.translation: Better to do your own duty imperfectly than someone else's duty perfectly. Doing the work that comes from your own nature, you don't incur sin.

verses.sanskrit: सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत्।सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः।।18.48।।
verses.translation: Don't abandon the duty you are born to, Arjuna, even if it is flawed. Every undertaking is wrapped in some flaw, as fire is wrapped in smoke.

verses.sanskrit: असक्तबुद्धिः सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः।नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां संन्यासेनाधिगच्छति।।18.49।।
verses.translation: With an intellect unattached everywhere, with the self under control, free from desire — through renunciation one attains the supreme perfection of freedom from action.

verses.sanskrit: सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे।समासेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा।।18.50।।
verses.translation: How, once perfected, one reaches Brahman — the supreme culmination of knowledge — hear from me in brief, Arjuna.

verses.sanskrit: बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्याऽऽत्मानं नियम्य च।शब्दादीन् विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च।।18.51।।
verses.translation: With a pure intellect, restraining the self by firmness, giving up sound and the other sense-objects, setting aside attraction and aversion;

verses.sanskrit: विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः।ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः।।18.52।।
verses.translation: living in solitude, eating lightly, controlling body, speech, and mind, always intent on the yoga of meditation, taking refuge in dispassion;

verses.sanskrit: अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम्।विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते।।18.53।।
verses.translation: giving up ego, force, arrogance, desire, anger, and possession; free from "mine," at peace — one becomes fit to become Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति।समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम्।।18.54।।
verses.translation: Having become Brahman, serene in self, one neither grieves nor desires. The same toward all beings, one attains supreme devotion to me.

verses.sanskrit: भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः।ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम्।।18.55।।
verses.translation: Through devotion one comes to know me — how great I am, who I truly am. Knowing me in truth, one enters into me at once.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः।मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम्।।18.56।।
verses.translation: Performing all actions, always taking refuge in me, by my grace one reaches the eternal, imperishable abode.

verses.sanskrit: चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः।बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव।।18.57।।
verses.translation: Mentally surrendering all actions to me, with me as the highest goal, resorting to the yoga of the intellect — always fix your mind on me.

verses.sanskrit: मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि।अथ चेत्त्वमहङ्कारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि।।18.58।।
verses.translation: With your mind on me, you'll cross all difficulties by my grace. But if, out of ego, you won't listen, you'll be lost.

verses.sanskrit: यदहङ्कारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे।मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति।।18.59।।
verses.translation: If, clinging to ego, you think "I will not fight" — your resolve is useless. Your nature will compel you.

verses.sanskrit: स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा।कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत्।।18.60।।
verses.translation: Bound, Arjuna, by your own karma born of your nature, you will helplessly do what, out of confusion, you don't want to do.

verses.sanskrit: ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति।भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया।।18.61।।
verses.translation: The Lord lives in the hearts of all beings, Arjuna, making all beings spin as if mounted on a machine, by his illusory power.

verses.sanskrit: तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत।तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम्।।18.62।।
verses.translation: Take refuge in him alone, with all your being, Arjuna. By his grace you'll attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.

verses.sanskrit: इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया।विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु।।18.63।।
verses.translation: So the wisdom more secret than any secret has been told to you by me. Reflect on it fully — then do as you wish.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः श्रृणु मे परमं वचः।इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम्।।18.64।।
verses.translation: Listen again to my supreme word — the most secret of all. You are deeply loved by me; so I'll tell you, for your welfare.

verses.sanskrit: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे।।18.65।।
verses.translation: Fix your mind on me, be devoted to me, offer to me, bow to me. You'll come to me — truly, I promise — because you are dear to me.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः।।18.66।।
verses.translation: Let go of all dharmas and take refuge in me alone. I will free you from all sins. Do not grieve.

verses.sanskrit: इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन।न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति।।18.67।।
verses.translation: Never share this teaching with someone without discipline, without devotion, who doesn't want to listen, or who speaks ill of me.

verses.sanskrit: य इमं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति।भक्ितं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः।।18.68।।
verses.translation: Whoever teaches this supreme secret to my devotees, practicing supreme devotion to me, will without doubt come to me.

verses.sanskrit: न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्िचन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः।भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि।।18.69।।
verses.translation: Among humans, no one does more precious service to me than that person, and no one on earth will be more beloved by me.

verses.sanskrit: अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः।ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः।।18.70।।
verses.translation: And whoever studies this righteous dialogue between us — by them, I shall be worshipped through the sacrifice of knowledge. That's my conviction.

verses.sanskrit: श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च श्रृणुयादपि यो नरः।सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम्।।18.71।।
verses.translation: Even the person who, full of faith and without ill-will, simply listens to it — even they, liberated, will attain the auspicious worlds of the meritorious.

verses.sanskrit: कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा।कच्चिदज्ञानसंमोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय।।18.72।।
verses.translation: Have you heard this, Arjuna, with a one-pointed mind? Has the delusion born of your ignorance been destroyed?

verses.sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाचनष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत।स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव।।18.73।।
verses.translation:
Arjuna said:
My delusion is destroyed; I've regained my right understanding by your grace, Krishna. I stand free from doubt. I will do as you say.

verses.sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाचइत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः।संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम्।।18.74।।
verses.translation:
Sanjaya said:
Thus I heard this dialogue between Krishna and the noble Arjuna — wonderful, making the hair stand on end.

verses.sanskrit: व्यासप्रसादाच्छ्रुतवानेतद्गुह्यमहं परम्।योगं योगेश्वरात्कृष्णात्साक्षात्कथयतः स्वयम्।।18.75।।
verses.translation: By the grace of Vyasa, I heard this supreme secret of yoga directly from Krishna, the Lord of yoga, who declared it in person.

verses.sanskrit: राजन्संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य संवादमिममद्भुतम्।केशवार्जुनयोः पुण्यं हृष्यामि च मुहुर्मुहुः।।18.76।।
verses.translation: O king, as I recall again and again this wondrous and sacred dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna, I rejoice again and again.

verses.sanskrit:
तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः।

विस्मयो मे महान् राजन् हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः।।18.77।।
verses.translation: And as I remember again and again that most wondrous form of Hari (Krishna), my wonder is great, O king, and I rejoice again and again.

verses.sanskrit:
यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः।

तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम।।18.78।
verses.translation: Wherever Krishna is — the Lord of yoga — wherever Arjuna is — the wielder of the bow — there, I am convinced, are fortune, victory, prosperity, and sound judgment.
